Flop is 2h 8h 9h and I hold Qc2c — I’ve paired the deuce (bottom pair) with a decent queen kicker but I have no heart and I’m OOP. The board is dangerous (three hearts and connected), so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand. Checking for pot control and to deny free cards to heart draws is the right play: I’ll check and call small-to-medium continuation bets to realize equity, but fold to large raises or heavy turn aggression. If Elon checks back I’ll see a free turn and re-evaluate; if he bets small I’ll call and plan to fold to strong turn sizing unless I improve.

Flop is 2h8h9h, a highly coordinated monotone board hitting many draws (flush draws, straight draws like J10, T7). My Ac4s has ace-high with no pair, no draw, but blocks the nut flush (Ah) and some Ax. As preflop raiser from button vs BB caller, my range is perceived stronger: more suited broadways, Ax suited, suited connectors with hearts. Minni's BB calling range is wider but capped here without top pair+. She checked, showing weakness. Pot 650, position advantage. Standard continuation bet to deny equity to her draws/speculative hands, protect ace-high, and take it down often. Sizing ~60% pot (400) balances value (overpairs, sets) and bluffs, puts pressure on her 46BB stack without overcommitting. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws that beat ace-high.

I called preflop to see a cheap flop with Qc2c and now have bottom pair (the 2) with a decent queen kicker on a very wet heart board (2h 8h 9h). Elon’s 400 into a 1050 pot is a small/medium c‑bet (~38% pot). This is exactly the spot I planned to call: raising OOP with a marginal hand has poor fold equity and folding is too weak given my pair and the price. Calling gives ~28% pot odds, keeps worse hands and bluffs in his range (missed broadways, single hearts, small pairs), and controls the pot size. Plan: call the flop, see the turn, and fold to large turn aggression unless I improve to trips/boats or the turn is a safe blank (non-heart) and he bets small — otherwise proceed cautiously and avoid bloating the pot out of position.

Check. I have bottom pair (2c) with a Q kicker on a very wet board: 2h 8h 9h As. The Ace turn hits many hands in Elon’s wide open/c-bet range (Ax, A9/A8, and multiple two‑heart combos that already had the flop flush). Betting OOP with a marginal made hand here is a losing line — poor fold equity and I’ll get called by better hands or fold out only the very worst. I’ll keep the pot under control: check and call small/medium turn bets to realize equity, fold to large/committal sizing, and re-evaluate on the river (if Elon checks back I’ll see a free river and decide then).

Turn brings the As, pairing the top of my range and giving me top pair (Ac) with a 4 kicker on a scary, draw-heavy board (2h8h9hAs). This improves my hand significantly from ace-high to top pair, while blocking strong Ax hands like AQs/AJs suited hearts. Minni's flop call after checking suggests she has showdown value (88/99/JT/T7 draws, heart flush draws like KhTh/QhTh) or medium pairs, but her turn check indicates weakness—no top pair likely, as she'd lead or raise with strong hands. Her stack is 4200 (~3x pot), vulnerable to pressure. Betting ~60% pot (900) extracts value from her worse pairs/draws that missed the turn ace, denies equity to remaining draws (e.g., heart flush still open but less potent), and protects against runner-runner threats. Checking risks giving a free river to beats like flushes/straights. Position allows control if she shoves/jams.

Board is very wet (2h 8h 9h As) and I have bottom pair (2c) with a decent queen kicker. Elon is a wide button opener who c-bet the flop and again bet the turn 900 into a 2350 pot — a small/medium sizing that will include both value hands (A‑x, 9s/8s, sets, made hearts) and some bluffs. Raising out of position with a marginal hand like this has poor fold equity and often commits me with an inferior hand, while folding here gives up too much equity and folds out the weaker parts of his range. Calling 900 to see the river keeps worse hands and bluffs in, controls the pot size (I only risk 900 of my 4200 stack), and lets me re-evaluate on the river: fold to a large river shove or big value bet if the board becomes scarier or he polarizes; value-bet/raise only if I improve to trips/two‑pair or he gives a clear check/weak-line. For these reasons I will call the 900.

Pot is 3,250 and I’m OOP with Qc2c — bottom pair (deuce) with a decent kicker. Elon opened BTN, c-bet the wet heart flop and barreled the Ace turn with relatively small/medium sizes; he then checked the river (6s). That line reads as a lot of showdown/pot-control hands (Ax, medium pairs like 9/8, some slowplayed made hands) and some missed draws. My hand has some showdown value but very little fold equity — a river bet will mostly get called by hands that beat me and only fold the pure air. I’m also a short-ish stack (≈33bb) and don’t want to bloat a pot OOP with a marginal hand. Best line is to check and take it to showdown; I only lose to better hands but avoid risking more chips on a low-EV bluff or thin value bet.

River 6s is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete many new draws (spade flush unlikely given no prior spades, straight unlikely without specific 10-7). Board remains draw-heavy with open-ended possibilities and heart flush, but Minni has checked flop, turn, and river, indicating capped range: likely missed heart draw (e.g., KQh/JTh, single heart), middle pair (88/99), or weak ace (A2-A7, but blocked somewhat by my Ac). My Ac4s is top pair aces (pairing the As) with a weak 4-kicker, ahead of her likely bluffs/draws/middle pairs. Position, initiative from preflop raiser, and her passive check-calls suggest thin value bet extracts from worse showdown value or fold equity on missed draws. Pot 3250, her stack 3300 (~1x pot), so bet ~60% pot (2000) pressures her short stack, gets called by 88/99/weak A/missed draw with showdown, maximizes value without overbetting into monsters (which she'd likely bet/raise earlier). Checking risks her checking behind weaker hands we beat.

Elon ships a large river bet (2,000 into a ~3,250 pot). Calling 2,000 to win a final pot of ~7,250 requires ~27.6% equity (2,000 / 7,250). I have Qc2c — bottom/second pair on a very wet heart/connected board (2h 8h 9h As 6s). Elon has been firing flop and turn and now produces a big river sizing: that line is consistent with a value-heavy range (Ax, 9x/8x, sets, flopped flushes and some two-pair/two-card combos). There simply aren’t enough believable bluffs in his line to give my thin pair the ~28% equity needed, and calling would commit a huge portion of my ~3,300 stack (leaving ~1,300). Raising all‑in as a bluff OOP is close to pure gamble — low fold equity versus his likely range and disastrous if called. For stack preservation and long-term EV, I fold and preserve chips for better spots.

I am on the button with a pocket pair of Threes (3c 3d). In a three-handed game, any pair is reasonably strong pre-flop. My goal with small pairs is usually to see a cheap flop and try to hit a set, or take the pot down early. Minni is short-stacked with only 3250 chips. A standard raise to 300 is effective here to build the pot and potentially take the blinds, while staying prepared to fold if there is significant resistance from Elon's deep stack.

I have K♠Q♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 in a 3-handed game. KQo is a strong hand heads-up/3-handed and the button's opening range is relatively wide. Calling (250 more) would leave me out of position vs both opponents and invite a multiway pot where KQ loses equity and initiative. A min-raise to 500 looks weak and gives little fold equity. I want to take the initiative, isolate the button, and get value from worse broadways/medium pairs, so I’ll 3-bet to a standard size (~3x the open). Raising to 900 (total) achieves that: it gives fold equity, narrows fields, and leaves me ~2400 behind to play postflop. If I get 4-bet I’ll re-evaluate — likely fold to a large 4-bet given position and stack dynamics, but I’m happy to continue if called. Action: raise to 900.

Preflop in BB with Ad3h offsuit facing button raise to 300 and SB 3-bet to 900. Pot 1300, call 800. A3o is a weak ace with poor kicker, dominated by better aces and low equity against typical 3-bet ranges (e.g., QQ+, AK). Pot odds require ~38% equity for call, but A3o has ~25-30% vs strong 3-bet range. Poor postflop playability multiway or heads-up, especially with Minni's short stack (2400) likely to continue aggressively. Folding preserves stack for better spots in deep-stack game.

Minni's 3-bet to 900 represents a significant portion of her remaining stack (she has 2400 left). With an SPR of approximately 1.25 on the flop if I call, I would be forced into a high-variance situation. My pocket threes are unlikely to improve (approx. 12% chance to hit a set), and the implied odds are not sufficient to justify calling for set-mining alone given her short stack. In a 3-handed game, while 33 has some value, it is often crushed by a short-stack's 3-betting range which likely consists of higher pairs or strong overcards. I will fold and maintain my significant chip lead.

I’m on the button with Q♣5♦ — a marginal, easily dominated hand but playable in position. The price is cheap: calling 100 into a 150 pot lets me see a flop with positional advantage and keep the pot small. A min-raise here with Q5o is thin and will often get called or 3‑bett by better hands; folding is overly tight given the odds. Plan: call and evaluate the flop — look to c-bet small if checked to and I pick up equity (a Q or good turn), otherwise fold to heavy aggression. If faced with a substantial preflop 3‑bet I’ll fold.

K4o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity multiway out of position. Facing a button limp, completing the SB for 50 into 250 offers poor implied odds given my position OOP postflop against Minni and potential BB action. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in deepstack play.

I have 9c 6c in the big blind. Minni limped from the button and Elon folded. Since the action is back to me and I can see a flop for free, checking is the most sensible play with a speculative suited hand like 96s. There is no need to bloat the pot pre-flop against a short stack like Minni with this holding.

I am in the Big Blind in a limped pot with 9c 6c. The flop is 7s 2s 2d, which is a paired board that likely missed both of us given the pre-flop action. I have 9-high and no direct draw. In a small, limped pot, there is no reason to lead out with a bet here. Checking is the standard play to see how the button (Minni) reacts and potentially see a free turn card.

Flop is 7♠ 2♠ 2♦. I have Q♣5♦ — queen-high, no pair and no spade draw. I’m on the button and in position; Flash checked the flop, so his range contains a lot of missed hands and weak holdings that a small c-bet can fold out. This board is relatively dry (paired low board) so a small, credible bet should win the pot a good percentage of the time and keeps the pot manageable if I get called. Plan: make a small c-bet to take it down or get a cheap turn decision. If I face a sizable raise I’ll fold — my hand has poor showdown value and little equity against strong hands. If called, I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue with improvement or a very cheap/weak action from opponent. Bet size chosen to pressure floats and unpaired overcards while risking only a small portion of my 3,600 stack.

The flop is 7s 2s 2d, a paired board that I have completely missed with my 9c 6c. Minni has bet 100 into a 200 pot. While the price to call is small, I have no equity, no draws, and only 9-high. Minni's button limp followed by a flop bet is a common play with any two cards, but without any potential to improve or a strong reason to float, I should not waste chips. Folding is the most disciplined choice here.

T3o is a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high cards. Even in position on the button in a 3-handed game, it has very poor playability and equity against calling ranges. Opponents have solid stacks, especially Minni short but still not worth entering a pot OOP with this. Folding preserves stack for better spots to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

I am in the Small Blind with pocket deuces (2d 2c). The Button (Elon) has folded. I am facing the Big Blind (Minni), who has a relatively short stack of 3750 chips. Pocket pairs, even the lowest ones, are strong hands in blind-vs-blind situations. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially take it down pre-flop. If Minni calls or reraises, I will evaluate based on the flop texture or the size of her 3-bet.

Flash (SB) opened to 300. I’m in the BB with J♦7♠ and a 3750 stack (~37.5bb). J7o is too marginal to 3-bet for fold equity here and a raise to the min (500) commits more chips than I want with a raggy hand against an aggressive opener with a deep stack. Calling the extra 200 (300 total committed including my posted BB) gives good pot odds (~2:1) to see a flop and lets me realize equity and play postflop: continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy pressure when I miss. Therefore I’ll defend the BB and call.

I am the pre-flop aggressor with pocket deuces. The flop of Qc 6h 3h contains one overcard to my pair. A standard continuation bet is appropriate here to maintain the lead and put pressure on Minni's range, which likely includes many unpaired overcards. I'll bet 300, which is half the pot, to see how she responds. If she raises, I can safely fold knowing my underpair is likely beaten.

I defended the BB preflop with J♦7♠ to see a cheap flop, but on Qc‑6h‑3h I’ve missed: no pair, no heart, and only 6 realistic outs to pair (three Js and three 7s) — about ~24% to improve by river, which is slightly below the ~25% equity needed to call 300 into a 900 pot. Flash is an aggressive opener who will apply further pressure if I call, and a raise would be too loose and commit too many chips with this rag. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am on the button with KQs, which is a premium starting hand in a three-handed game. My stack is very healthy at 16,750 chips. Minni is in the small blind with a relatively short stack of 3,500, while Elon is in the big blind with 19,600. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and put pressure on the blinds.

I’m in the small blind with 3♠3♣ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 250 more (total 300). With 35 BB effective, a small pocket pair is best played by set-mining here rather than inflating a pot out of position with a marginal hand. 3-betting or shoving commits too much and opens me to 4-bets/calls from deeper stacks; calling preserves stack and keeps my options open. If I hit a set I can extract value; if I miss I’ll play cautiously and fold to heavy aggression. I’ll call to see a flop (total 300).

97 suited clubs from BB facing a button raise to 300 and SB call of 250. Pot is 700, calling 200 gives excellent pot odds of 3.5:1 (need ~22% equity). 97s has great playability: suited for flush potential, connectors for straight draws. Against loose button range (Flash aggressive from history) and calling station SB (Minni), multiway pot is fine with deep stacks (mine 19.6k, Flash 16k). Folding too tight preflop; raising risks isolating vs strong hands or bloating pot OOP multiway. Call to realize equity postflop.

I called preflop with 3♠3♣ to set‑mine and I missed the flop: Q♦ 5♠ 2♠. This is a three‑way pot (button opened, BB defended) and I have very little showdown value — I’m behind any Q,5,2 pair or any overpair. Betting into two players here bloats the pot, gets called by better hands, and burns my implied odds if I do hit a set. I’ll check to control the pot and preserve my 32.5BB stack. Plan: if I hit a 3 on the turn I’ll extract value (lead/raise); if I miss and face a small bet (≈ ≤1/3 pot ≈ 300) I’ll call to continue set‑mining, but I’ll fold to larger sizing (>≈1/2 pot ≈ 450). If both opponents check the turn I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn card. For now: check.

Flop misses me completely: no pair, no flush draw (clubs don't hit), no straight draw (97 doesn't connect with Q52). Pure air in multiway pot OOP. Minni's check suggests weakness, but betting risks Flash raising with Qx/draw/spades, and I have no fold equity against short-stacked Minni (3250 behind) who might shove wide. Checking keeps pot small, realizes equity on speculative hand called preflop multiway against aggressive button (Flash) and calling SB (Minni). Pot odds were good pre, continue realizing equity cheaply. Turn/river might improve or Flash/Minni show down weak.

I have top pair with a King kicker on a board of Qd-5s-2s. The pot is 900 and both opponents have checked. I should bet here to get value from weaker Queens, flush draws (two spades are on the board), and mid-pairs. A bet of around half to two-thirds the pot is appropriate to build the pot while protecting against draws. I will bet 500.

I called preflop to set‑mine with 3♠3♣, but I completely missed this flop (Q♦ 5♠ 2♠). Flash now bets 500 into the ~900 pot (~55% pot) — the total pot is 1,400 and my cost to continue is 500. My preflop plan was to call small bets to continue set‑mining but fold to larger sizings (~>1/2 pot ≈450). Pot odds to call are only ~26%, while my chance to hit a 3 on the turn is 2/47 ≈4.3% (≈8.4% by river) — far too small to justify calling or investing more in a multiway, out‑of‑position spot. Raising as a bluff is thin here (multiway, I block nothing, and both opponents can realistically have Q/x or spades). Preserving my 32.5BB stack for better spots is the best long‑term play, so I’ll fold.

Facing a 500 bet into a 1400 pot on Qd5s2s flop with 9c7c (no pair, no draws). Equity needed ~26% but 97cc has minimal equity vs Flash's wide button c-bet range (Qx, broadway, spades, Ax, bluffs). Checked preflop realizing equity multiway cheaply OOP, but Minni folded leaving heads-up vs aggressive Button with position. No showdown value, no strong draws, backdoors insignificant. Raising has no fold equity deep-stacked OOP; calling bloats pot OOP with air. Standard fold to avoid realizing 0 equity long-term.

I’m on the button with J♠7♥ (a marginal but playable late-position hand) and have 32.5bb effective. Facing only the blinds with 150 in the pot and 100 to call, I want to take the initiative and try to steal the blinds or play the flop in position. A 3x open (to 300 total) is a standard, bully size here: it’s big enough to reduce the chance of getting multi-way callers but not so large that it commits too much of my stack. Plan: if both players fold I pick up the pot; if I’m called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on uncoordinated/overcards. If I face a sizable 3-bet (e.g., ~700+), I’ll fold this hand preflop — J7o is not worth getting tangled up against strong aggression out of position.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (needing 250 to call into a 450 pot). My hand is 2s6s, low suited gappers with poor playability out of position post-flop. While suited connectors can make strong hands, this specific hand has low equity against a typical button raising range (which includes many strong hands and bluffs). Calling OOP risks difficult spots with marginal equity, and the pot odds (about 1.8:1) don't justify it given reverse implied odds if I hit. Raising is not viable without position or a premium hand. Folding preserves stack for better spots, aligning with long-term EV maximization over 150 hands. Historical opponent tendencies (Minni aggressive) don't change this weak holding.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack of Hearts and Seven of Diamonds. The button, Minni, has raised to 300. Minni is currently the short stack with only 2,950 remaining. Given the pot odds, I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 450. However, J7 offsuit is a relatively weak hand and does not play particularly well post-flop, especially out of position against a player who is likely looking for a spot to commit their remaining stack. Looking at Minni's recent history, she has been aggressive when entering pots. While I am getting decent odds, folding here preserves my stack and avoids a potentially awkward post-flop situation against a short-stacked opponent.

Preflop on the button with 9d5c, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against any range. Pot is 150 with to-call 100, indicating a raise ahead, likely from SB or BB. Even in position, this hand is not worth calling or raising given opponents' stacks and history of aggressive play. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Small Blind with Q5o. The action has folded to me. Minni is in the Big Blind with a relatively short stack of 3300 chips (33 big blinds). Q5o is a weak hand, but in a blind-vs-blind situation, it has some value. However, Minni has been playing aggressively in previous hands. Given the stack sizes and the pot odds (calling 50 more to win 150), I could complete, but folding is also a safe option with such a weak hand out of position. Actually, with only 50 to call, it is worth seeing a flop, but a small raise to test Minni's short stack might be more effective. On second thought, simply folding avoids potential trouble against an aggressive short stack.

I am on the button with King-Queen of Clubs, which is a premium starting hand in a three-handed game. My stack is very deep (17,200), whereas Minni is relatively short-stacked (3,400). Elon is the deep-stack opponent. Standard play on the button is to raise to gain initiative and potentially take down the blinds or play a pot in position. I will raise to 300.

I’m in the small blind with 5♦7♣ facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I must call 250 (300 total). 5‑7 offsuit has very poor equity against a button’s open range, and those pot-odds (~250 into a 700 pot if I call, ≈36%) are higher than this hand’s realistic equity — especially if Elon (BB) comes along and it goes multiway. Reraising to 500 would be a light bluff out of position with little fold equity and unnecessary risk with 34bb effective. Prefer to preserve stack and wait for a better spot, so fold.

Preflop in BB facing button raise to 300 after SB fold. Pot 450, calling 200 more (great odds ~2.25:1). K2s is suited with king blocker, decent playability for flush/straight draws or top pair. Flash likely wide button range from history of frequent button aggression. Defend wide OOP with position post-flop implied odds deep stacks (19k effective). Will play cautiously post-flop, folding to c-bet without improvement but floating/raising draws as needed. Folding too tight, 3-betting too strong for this hand.

Flop of 6h Tc 9d completely misses my K2s suited. No pair, no flush draw (no spades), no straight draw (K2 doesn't connect with 6-9-T). Pure king-high with poor playability OOP first to act vs aggressive button raiser who will c-bet wide ranges frequently based on history. Defending preflop was fine for BB odds and implied odds, but no improvement means check-fold to any reasonable bet size. No reason to bluff raise without fold equity or blockers to opponent's value. Preserve stack for better spots.

I raised pre-flop with King-Queen of Clubs and was called by Elon in the Big Blind. The flop of 10-9-6 is somewhat coordinated but doesn't hit my hand directly, though I do have two overcards and a backdoor club draw. Elon has checked to me. While this board favors his defending range slightly (hands like 78, 98, JT, etc.), I should maintain my initiative with a continuation bet. A bet here can take the pot down immediately or set up a barrel on certain turn cards (King, Queen, or any club). I will bet roughly half the pot.

K2s completely misses the 6h-Tc-9d flop: no pair, no flush draw (spades didn't hit), no straight draw or even gutshot. Pure king-high with backdoor spade potential at best, but OOP facing a half-pot c-bet from an aggressive button raiser who frequently c-bets wide ranges. Preflop defend was correct for BB odds and dead money, but without improvement, check-fold preserves stack. Pot odds ~3.8:1 require ~20% equity, but we have <10% vs Flash's polarized button range (high pairs, sets, two-pair, draws like 87/J8, bluffs). No blockers to value hands, poor playability for floats OOP. Deep stacks don't justify a bluff call here with no fold equity or draw. Standard fold to avoid spew.

I’m on the button with A♢7♠ in a 3-way pot. This is a worthwhile hand to open-raise from the button: good positional advantage, decent blocker to other A‑x hands, and playable postflop. Pot is 150 and the call is 100; I’ll use a standard 3xBB-ish open to 300 to steal the blinds and define ranges. 300 is table‑standard sizing here, preserves fold equity, and is a small enough commitment with my 3,400 stack (~34 BB) to fold to a large 3‑bet but continue postflop when I hit or have good equity. If faced with a big 3‑bet (>~800–1,000) I’ll fold; versus a small 3‑bet I may call depending on exact sizing and opponent tendencies. Postflop plan: c‑bet favorable dry boards, realize equity with draws, and play cautiously on heavy action when I don’t improve.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). Holding 8s5h, an offsuit hand with poor connectivity, low cards, and no high-card strength. This is a clear fold against a button opening range, which is wide but still heavily favors playable hands. Out of position postflop with such a weak hand offers no playability or bluffing equity. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Pot odds are about 1.8:1, but equity realization is too low (~20-30% vs typical button range).

I am in the Big Blind with 8d 5d. Minni, who has a relatively short stack of 3,100, has raised to 300 from the Button. My hand has some post-flop playability as a suited connector-style hand, and I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 450 (getting 3.25:1 odds). Given the pot odds and the fact that we are playing 3-handed, defending the Big Blind with this hand is standard to see a flop.

On this flop of 6c-9d-7h, I have an open-ended straight draw (5-6-7-8-9) and one diamond. The board is quite connected and hits a lot of a Big Blind defending range. Minni, who raised pre-flop, has checked behind or hasn't acted yet? Wait, Minni raised to 300 pre-flop and I called. Now it's the flop and I am first to act. This board is very good for my range. I have the open-ender. I will check to the raiser to see how she responds, keeping my options open to check-call or check-raise depending on her bet size.

I have A♦7♠ on 6♣9♦7♥ — a middle pair (pair of sevens) with an Ace kicker. I was the preflop aggressor and the BB checked, so a standard continuation bet is appropriate: the board is fairly dry with few clean draws, and a half‑pot c‑bet will get value from worse pairs (6x, small pockets) and charge any backdoor draws while keeping the pot controllable. I’ll size to about half the pot (~350) — big enough to fold out pure air and gain value, but small enough that I won’t be overly committed if I face strong resistance. If I get a large check‑raise I’ll fold (I don’t want to commit with only middle pair); if called I’ll re‑evaluate the turn and barrel with improvements or choose pot‑control versus heavy action.

On a board of 6c-9d-7h, I have an open-ended straight draw (5-6-7-8-9). Minni has bet 350 into a 650 pot. With a 350 call to win 1350, I am getting approximately 3.8:1 odds. Given that I have 8 outs to a straight (and any 5 or 8 gives me a piece, though 8 makes the straight), this is a mandatory call. A check-raise is also an option given the board texture favors the Big Blind defender range, but Minni's stack is quite short (2750 remaining), making a call more prudent to see the turn and potentially trap or fold if the board gets too dangerous without hitting.

On the turn, the 2s is a total brick and doesn't change the board state. I still have an open-ended straight draw (any 5 or 10 gives me a straight). The pot is 1350 and I am first to act. Since I called the flop, checking is the standard move to see how Minni proceeds. She has about 2750 left in her stack. If she bets, I will have to evaluate the odds, but for now, checking is the best play to realize my equity or potentially induce a bluff from a weaker hand.

I’m in position with A♦7♠ — I have a single pair (sevens) with a good kicker but not a hand I want to build a big pot with. The turn 2♠ is essentially a blank and doesn’t complete obvious draws; the pot is already 1,350 and my stack is ~2,750, so a turn bet would risk committing too much against hands that beat me (9x, overpairs, sets) or getting raised off my equity. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me realize my equity in position, and allows villain to bluff or show weakness on the river. Plan: check-call modest river bets for value or fold to large aggression unless I improve or get a very specific read to call.

I have a 9-high straight (5-6-7-8-9), which I actually made on the flop. I have played the hand very deceptively so far by check-calling the flop and checking the turn. Minni's check on the turn suggests she has a marginal hand with showdown value (like a pair of nines or sevens) or she has given up on a bluff. Since I am out of position and the turn went check-check, I need to bet now to extract value. A bet of 900 into a 1350 pot (approx 2/3 pot) is a standard value-bet sizing that can be called by any one-pair hand or even a stubborn Ace-high.

I have A♦7♠ — one pair (sevens) with the best possible kicker against lots of single‑pair or missed hands. Flash called my flop c‑bet and checked the turn, then fires 900 on the river into a 1,350 pot (pot is 2,250 after the bet). Calling 900 requires ~28.6% equity (900 / (2250+900)), which is reasonable for my hand in position against a wide calling/catching range (missed broadways, A‑x, K‑x, small pairs). Hands that definitely beat me are mostly sets or unlikely slow‑played overpairs; many of Flash’s flop‑call hands are actually behind. Raising would polarize and either fold out worse hands or get called/raised by better hands — I don’t want to bloat the pot with a single pair. Given the pot odds, position, and my kicker, I’ll call to reach showdown.

8s5c is a trash hand: offsuit, gapped low cards, no high card strength or playability. Even on the button in a limped pot (to call 100 into 150, ~1.5:1), it's not worth entering with such a weak range. Minni is short at 1750 but still, no speculative value here versus two opponents, one deep (Flash). Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid dominated spots or tough post-flop decisions.

I am in the Small Blind with a small pocket pair (2h 2d). Elon has folded, and I am facing a call of 50 to see the flop or I can raise against Minni. Minni's stack is quite short (1750), while I have a massive lead (19100). Given the small effective stack of 17.5 big blinds for Minni, pocket deuces are strong enough to raise and put pressure on her or potentially call if she jams. A standard raise to 300 is appropriate to try and take the pot immediately or isolate.

I'm the big blind with JhQc (QJo offsuit) facing a small-blind open to 300. Pot is 400 and I need to call 200 (effective stack ~1750 ≈ 17.5bb). QJo is too strong to fold heads-up and gets well above the ~25% equity required by the ~3:1 immediate pot odds, so calling is the +EV play. A minimum 3-bet to 500 is an option, but with my stack size and being out of position postflop a small 3-bet won't yield much fold equity and a larger 3-bet or shove is too committal with this hand. I will call to see a flop and play postflop based on texture (continue with top pair/strong draws, fold to heavy pressure on unfavorable boards).

I am the pre-flop aggressor and have a small pocket pair (2h 2d) on a paired board of 4c 4s 9c. This is generally a good board to continuation bet as it is hard for the Big Blind (Minni) to have hit strongly, unless she has a 4 or a 9. Given Minni's short stack (1550), a half-pot bet of 300 puts significant pressure on her. If she calls or raises, I can re-evaluate, but usually, I can take the pot down right here against most of her range.

Facing a 300 c-bet into a ~900 pot on 4c 4s 9c with JhQc. I have no pair, only a backdoor club draw and 6 immediate outs (Q/J) — roughly ~24% to pair by river and ~4% for the backdoor club flush, so my combined chance to improve is around the ~25–28% range. That lines up with the pot odds for a 300 call. Raising OOP with no pair and little fold equity would be too committal and puts me in tough spots vs a wide calling range. Plan: call to realize equity, fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss, and extract value if I pair or pick up strong club help.

The Ten of Clubs is a poor turn card for my pocket deuces. It completes potential club flushes and any Ten in Minni's range now beats me, in addition to any Nine or Four. Since Minni is short-stacked with only about one pot-sized bet remaining, I will check to see her reaction. I am likely behind or facing significant equity from her overcards, and I don't want to commit more chips to a hand that has likely lost its value as the aggressor.

Board: 4c 4s 9c Tc. My hand: Qc Jh. Pot 1200, my stack 1250. I have a high-card Q club plus a four-card club draw (Qc + 4c/9c/Tc) and two overcards (Q/J) — roughly 14 unique outs (~30% to improve on the river). Flash checked the turn after c-betting the flop, which looks like weakness or pot control. A half-pot/medium-sized semi-bluff is the best balance here: it applies pressure and can take the pot now, but if called I still have good equity to realize. Also, with ~30% equity I can bet up to about ~930 and still be +EV if called, so a 600 bet keeps the called-case profitable while preserving fold equity. I will bet 600 to fold weaker hands and realize equity when called; if he raises big I’ll re-evaluate (fold to large jam unless the sizing/reads justify a call).

On the turn, the Ten of Clubs (Tc) is a very dangerous card. It completes a potential club flush and gives Minni's range many more ways to beat my pocket deuces (any Ten, any Nine, any Four, or any flush). Minni has bet 600, leaving her with only 650 chips behind. This is effectively an all-in situation. My pocket deuces are now essentially a bluff-catcher against a very narrow range of semi-bluffs or air, but given the board texture, it's more likely she has connected or is drawing to a flush that has me crushed. I checked to control the pot, and her bet indicates strength. I will fold and preserve my large stack.

I am on the button with 92o. This is a very weak hand. While the stacks are such that I have a significant lead over Minni, 92o is not a hand I want to open or play from the button, especially since I am already in the game for 0 additional chips (except for the ante if there were any, but here it's just the blinds). In a standard 3-handed game, 92o is a clear fold to avoid unnecessary losses with a junk hand.

I have A♦Q♥ in the small blind heads-up vs the big blind — a strong hand that I want to play aggressively. Raising isolates the BB, gains fold equity against his wide defending range, and lets me take the initiative postflop. With a 2.4k stack (~24 BB) I want a standard, reasonably sized open: 3x the big blind (300 total) is big enough to price out speculative hands but keeps the pot controllable. If Elon 3-bets large I'll re-evaluate based on sizing; otherwise I'll c-bet favorable flops and extract value. Raise to 300 (total).

Preflop in BB facing a raise to 300 from short-stacked SB (21BB effective). Pot is 400, calling 200 more for excellent pot odds (2:1 direct, better implied). K5o blocks some of her strong king hands and has high-card strength against her wide stealing range from SB after BTN fold. OOP is a concern, but the price is too good to fold, and her short stack offers implied odds if we connect postflop (TPK possible). Raising risks bloating the pot or facing a shove where equity is lower. Calling keeps the pot small and allows controlled play postflop.

I opened preflop with A♦Q♥ and the flop J♣‑3♠‑5♦ is very dry and favorable for a continuation bet. As the preflop aggressor I have range advantage and two overcards + an Ace blocker, so a c‑bet will fold out many of his marginal calling hands and gain info. I’m sizing about half pot (300 into a 600 pot) to apply pressure while keeping the pot controllable — if he folds I take it down, if he calls I can evaluate the turn and float or barrel selectively; I’ll fold to a large flop raise unless the sizing or reads suggest a bluff.

Preflop called wide SB raise OOP with K5o for good odds and blockers. Flop pairs our 5 for middle pair with live K kicker on a dry J-3-5 rainbow board. Minni's c-bet is small (1/3 pot) from her short 18.5BB stack, representing her wide stealing range including Ax, Kx, suited connectors, bluffs. Our hand blocks some of her Kx value and has showdown value. Draws are minimal (few gutshots). Raising extracts value from her worse pairs/draws/overcards, charges them, denies equity, and leverages her short stack for max value before she can realize equity on later streets. Minraise too small; sizing to ~3x for pot control and fold equity if she has air. OOP fine here as we dictate pot size and she commits.

I opened preflop with A♦Q♥ and c‑bet 300 on a very dry J♣‑3♠‑5♦ flop. Elon check‑raised to 900, so I must call 600 into a 1800 pot (or raise to 1500, which would commit nearly my whole stack). Pot odds to call are ~25% (600/(1800+600)). The line and sizing are consistent with a strong value range on this texture (Jx, pocket pairs, sets, maybe some two‑pair/overpairs) — hands against which AQs has well under ~25% equity on average. I do have an Ace blocker, but not enough to make calling or jam‑raising +EV here. Raising to 1500 would be too thin and effectively shove most of my stack with poor equity. With no read that Elon is turning this into a frequent bluff, the correct, lower‑variance play is to fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with 7♠8♦ and 18.5bb effective. This is a playable, position-dependent hand but I don’t want to limp and invite multiway action against two very deep, aggressive blinds. I’ll open to a standard 3x (300) to (1) pick up the blinds often, (2) isolate a single defender and use position postflop, and (3) keep fold equity without committing my short stack by shoving. If I face a large 3‑bet shove I’ll fold (hand is too marginal to call off against a shove), but heads‑up or single-call situations give me good playability to continue as the aggressor.

Preflop in SB with K9o facing a button raise to 3BB from Minni. K9o is offsuit, marginal hand with poor playability OOP. Button range is wide but profitable vs it postflop is low equity when OOP. Pot odds ~1.8:1 but calling commits us OOP with a hand that plays poorly, better to fold and wait for better spots. My deep stack allows patience.

I am in the Big Blind with T6s, which is a reasonable hand to defend against a Button raise, especially given Minni's very short stack (1,550 total). She has already committed 300, leaving only 1,250 behind. Folding is too tight getting over 2:1 odds. While T6s is a decent speculative hand, I don't want to raise and potentially face an all-in from such a short stack unless I have a premium. Calling allows me to see a flop and realize my equity, and since Minni is so short, the effective stacks are very shallow, making the decision straightforward post-flop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Six of hearts. The flop is Jack-Five-Deuce with one heart. I have no pair and no direct draw, only a very thin backdoor flush and straight possibilities. Minni is short-stacked with only 1,550 chips remaining. In this scenario, as the Big Blind against the pre-flop aggressor, the standard play is to check to the raiser and see how they react. I do not have enough equity to lead out with a bet here.

I opened preflop to 300 and the pot is 650 on 2♣ J♦ 5♥. With 7♠8♦ I have no pair and almost no immediate equity (only weak pair outs / runner-runner possibilities). As the preflop aggressor in position on a very dry, disconnected board I want to apply pressure and use my fold equity. I’ll make a small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) to try to take it down now while keeping the size low so I’m not overly committed if I face a raise. If Flash raises big I’ll fold; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn.

I am in the Big Blind holding Th6h on a Jd5h2c flop. The pre-flop raiser, Minni, has bet 200 into a pot of 650. Minni is very short-stacked with only 1,350 chips remaining after this bet. I have no pair and very limited potential beyond a backdoor flush draw. Calling would cost 200 to win 850, providing good immediate odds, but with such a short effective stack, Minni is likely to commit the rest of her chips on later streets if she has any piece of the board or even a strong overcard. Given I have zero direct equity and the effective stacks make it hard to bluff later, folding is the disciplined choice.

T6o is a marginal offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low playability. Even on the button facing what appears to be a folded SB and just the BB (pot 150, to-call 100), the equity against a wide BB defending range is insufficient to justify calling or raising. BB has a short stack of 2100 (~21BB), but shoving over a raise would be easy with better holdings, and I have no interest in committing deep stacks with this hand. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in this long 150-hand game. Opponent tendencies from history show aggressive play, but no need to speculate here.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace of Spades and Six of Diamonds. It is a three-handed game since Elon folded on the button. Minni is in the Big Blind with a very short stack of only 2100 chips (21 big blinds). In this situation, I want to apply pressure but also be aware that Minni is likely to commit her stack frequently given her shallow depth. Ace-six offsuit is ahead of a random hand. I will raise to 300 to try and take the blinds or induce a shove from a wider range of hands that I can then evaluate based on pot odds. Since Minni has only 2100 total, any raise I make is effectively targeting her entire stack.

I have K♠5♠ in the big blind facing a SB open to 300. I only need to put in 200 more (total committed will be 300, since I already posted the BB). K5s is marginal but has decent playability (king blocker, suited, backdoor flush/straight potential) and gets good pot odds here (~33%). I'm out of position and don't want to 3-bet light given my ~2100 stack and the minimum raise-to 500 being relatively large. I'll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously — fold to heavy aggression if I miss.

On the flop of Js Qd 3d, I have Ace-high with a backdoor straight draw and one overcard to the board. The pot is 600 and my opponent, Minni, has a very short stack of 1900. While I don't have a piece of this board, it is a board that favors the pre-flop raiser's range. However, Minni is short-stacked and likely to call or shove with any pair or draw. Checking allows me to see how she reacts or potentially realize equity for free. If she checks behind, I might be able to take it down on a later street or improve.

I have K♠5♠ on Js Qd 3d — no pair and only weak backdoor spade/straight possibilities. Flash was the preflop raiser and checked the flop, so his range includes many Q/J/broadway hands and controlled or weak holdings. Betting OOP as a bluff here would often get called by better broadways and bloat a pot where I have very little equity, so I should control the pot size. I’ll check and concede the initiative; I can fold to a large c-bet, but I’ll consider calling a small probe to realize equity (chance to pair a K or hit spades) and see a cheaper turn.

On the turn, the Ace of Hearts gives me top pair. The board is coordinated with straight and flush possibilities (Js Qd 3d Ah). Since Minni checked back on the flop, she likely doesn't have a monster, but she could be holding a draw or a weaker pair. With her short stack of 1900, I want to start extracting value and protecting my hand against potential draws. I will bet 300, which is half the pot, to induce a call from worse hands or draws.

I’m OOP with K♠5♠ on Js Qd 3d Ah. Flash was the preflop raiser, checked the flop and now leads small (300 into a 900 pot). Calling 300 to win ~1200 requires ~25% equity. I only have ~6 clean outs (3 Ks + 3 5s) -> roughly 12–13% to hit the river, and many of those hits still lose to Ax, two pair or sets. Raising as a bluff is spewy OOP against the raiser and would commit a meaningful chunk of my ~19BB stack with very little fold equity. Given the math and opponent profile, this is a negative‑EV spot — I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

I am on the button with 8c 2s. This is a very weak starting hand, often referred to as 'trash'. While I have a large stack relative to Minni, who is in the Small Blind and very short-stacked (18.5 big blinds), Elon in the Big Blind has a stack comparable to mine. Given the poor quality of my hand and the fact that I'm facing two opponents, one of whom is desperate and likely to shove or call with any reasonable hand, there is no merit in playing this hand. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here.

I'm in the small blind with 9♦2♥ — a very weak, unsuited hand. Pot is 150 and it costs me 50 more to call (100 total committed). Heads-up ranges are wide, and the call is cheap: I get ~3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity) to see a flop. Raising to the 200 min would cost an extra 150 chips and is too spewy with this hand — opponent is deep-stacked and unlikely to fold enough, and I have poor postflop playability out of position. I'll call the 50 to see a flop, and plan to fold to significant aggression unless I pair or pick up real equity on the flop.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small blind limp from Minni, who has a short stack of 1800 (18BB). My hand is 7h8s, a suited connector with good playability and potential for strong postflop equity. Flash has folded, so heads-up. Checking is standard to realize equity in position postflop against a wide limping range from the SB. Raising risks bloating the pot OOP with a speculative hand, especially against a short stack that might commit. Pot odds are excellent to see a flop cheaply. Will play postflop based on board texture.

I flopped top pair (9♦) on 5♦-9♣-7♦. Heads-up vs the big blind’s limp/check range is wide and contains many worse pairs (7x,5x), small pockets and floats. Out of position I want to extract value and deny free cards to overcards and straight/runner-runner diamond possibilities, so a modest value bet is best. Betting ~50% pot (100 into the 200) charges draws, keeps worse hands and bluffs in, and gives me a clear plan: call small flats, fold to large check-raises unless I have a strong read. Bet 100.

Flop is 5d9c7d, giving me middle pair (7s) with an 8 kicker and a gutshot straight draw to the 6 (for 5-6-7-8-9). Solid equity against Minni's wide SB limping/betting range, which includes weak pairs, draws (like diamond flush draws, straight draws like 68/4-6/T8), Ax high, and dominated pairs. Her small 100 bet into ~200 pot (pot odds ~3:1) smells weak or trapping, but with position and her short 17BB stack (1700), raising builds the pot for value while protecting against draws and denies equity. Calling is too passive OOP for her—no, I have position postflop. Min raise is 200, but sizing to ~3.5x her bet (350 total) pressures her effectively without overcommitting; if she shoves, I'm ahead vs her range. Folding trash. History shows Minni limps wide and bets flops variably. Goal: extract value/maximize stack.

I c-bet 100 on 5♦-9♣-7♦ with top pair 9♦2♥ and Elon check‑raised to 350. That raise is polarized — sets/straights/two‑pair and some strong 9x hands, but also a share of bluffs/draws. Folding now surrenders value to bluffs and weaker pairs. Raising to 600 would commit too many chips with a very weak kicker and play poorly against the stronger portion of his range. Calling 250 is the cheapest, most flexible option: pot is 650 now, a 250 call makes it 900 and gives me ~28% pot‑odds to continue, which is reasonable to realize equity and keep bluffs/draws in. Plan: call and reassess on the turn — if I improve (9 trips) or he checks I’ll extract value; if he barrels big into a blank turn I can fold and preserve chips. Call 250.

I hold top pair (9♦) with a very poor 2 kicker on 5♦-9♣-7♦-Qs. Elon check‑raised my flop bet hard (350 over 100) — that line is polarized: sets, two‑pair, strong 9x, and some semi‑bluffs (diamond combos, 8‑6 straights). He then checked the turn, which is ambiguous: it can be a trap with a very strong hand or a cautious/missed‑draw line. The Queen on the turn is an overcard to my hand and improves many parts of his range (Qx), so my actual hand value is marginal. Pot is 900 and my remaining stack is 1,450 — I don't want to bloate the pot OOP with a weak kicker and run the risk of getting check‑raised and committed. I prefer pot control and to let villain bluff/declare on the river. Action: check and re‑evaluate on the river. If he bets a small size on the river I can call for value/bluff‑catch; a large turn/river sized bet from him will usually force a fold given my kicker.

On the turn Qs, the board is 5d9c7dQs. I have middle pair sevens (7h) with an 8 kicker and a gutshot straight draw to the 6 (5-6-7-8-9). This is a strong but vulnerable hand against Minni's wide SB limping/calling range, which includes weaker pairs (like 5x, low pairs), missed diamond/straight draws (68, T6, diamond Ax/draws), Ax high, or Qx that improved. She checked after calling my flop raise, suggesting weakness, possible missed draw, or trap with strong hand but unlikely given her history of limp-calling wide and small flop bet. Pot is 900, her stack is 1450 (~14.5BB), so she's pot-committed soon. Betting ~half-pot (450) gets value from her worse pairs/showdown hands, denies equity to any lingering draws, and builds the pot in case I hit my gutshot on river or improve. Checking risks giving free cards or allowing her to bluff river, but betting exploits her short stack and checked strength. Half-pot size induces calls from her ~30-40% equity hands while keeping fold equity if she has air. Position on river allows control if called. Long-term EV+ to bet for thin value/protection given history (she calls stations variably).

I have top pair (9♦) with a terrible kicker on 5♦-9♣-7♦-Qs. Elon’s check‑raise on the flop was strong and polarised (sets, two‑pair/straights, strong 9x, but also some semi‑bluffs). He now bets ~50% pot (450 into 900) after I checked — a size that keeps plenty of bluffs in his range. Folding here would be too tight given the pot odds (I need ~25% equity) and the likelihood he is continuing with some bluffs/draws and worse pairs; raising would commit too many chips OOP with a weak kicker. I’ll take the cheap, flexible line: call 450 to realize equity and re‑evaluate on the river. Plan on the river: fold to a large bet unless I improve (9 trips or a very clear bluff), call small bets for value/bluff‑catch, and raise only if I hit a very strong improvement.

I improved to trips on the river (9♦ in my hand + 9♣ and 9♠ on board). Elon’s line (check‑raise flop, continued aggression on the turn) is polarized — it contains strong made hands (sets, two‑pair, some Qx/9x) and some semi‑bluffs. Given my short 1,000 stack vs a 1,800 pot, trips is extremely strong and I should be trying to extract value rather than giving a free showdown. A half‑pot value bet (~900) targets the parts of his range I beat (single‑pair hands, worse 9x, missed draws that will call) while still leaving me comfortable to call a raise or shove all‑in if he jams. Betting 900 maximizes value from worse holdings, charges bluffs, and keeps the decision straightforward if he suddenly shows extreme strength. Action: bet 900.

River pairs the 9, improving me to two pair (9s and 7s, Q kicker), a strong hand that beats any unpaired hand, weaker two pairs (e.g., 9-5, 9-Q no, Q9 beats), A-high, missed draws. Minni's OOP short-stack (1000 pre-river) limped wide pre, small flop bet called my raise (likely TP9/7/5, diam flush draw, gutshot 68/46/T8), check-called turn (equity), now leads 900 (1/3 pot) leaving 100. Polarized range: value (9x trips, QQ/55/Q5/Q9 fulls ~10-20%), bluffs (missed ♦draw/gutshot, semi-bluffs turning into value-bluffs as pot-committed). Excellent pot odds (900 into 2700=3600 total, ~20% eq needed); her wide SB limp-call range bluffs enough here (shortstack first-to-act jam freq high). Blocks 7 (bottom pair), 8 (gutshot). Deep stack, call low-risk/high-EV vs history (Minni calls stations, wide). Crush bluffs/worse, pot odds crush value.

I'm on the button with 6♣3♥ — a very weak, unconnected offsuit hand. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see a flop (min-raise to 200 available). Calling 100 to win 250 requires roughly 40% equity just to breakeven one-on-one; in a three-handed pot my equity is far below that and I will be dominated or multiway faded on most flops. Raising to 200 with this hand is a thin steal against two deep, aggressive stacks and just invites a 3-bet or tough postflop spots with a garbage hand. With a 37BB stack I should preserve chips for better position/hand combinations — fold.

9h Th is a strong suited connector in the small blind facing a folded button and only the big blind behind. Deep stacks with position post-flop on the BB. Raising takes initiative, builds the pot with a hand that plays well multi-way or heads-up, and has good implied odds if we hit draws or pairs. Standard raise size to 3x the big blind. Folding or limping is too passive given the hand strength and dynamics.

I am in the Big Blind with 6d 5s, a weak connecting hand. The action folded to Elon in the Small Blind, who raised to 300. Given that I have to put in 200 more to call a pot of 400 (getting 2:1 odds) and we are very deep-stacked (174-184 BBs), calling is a marginal option, but 65o is generally too weak to defend against a standard SB open, even with position. Elon has shown aggression in previous hands and my hand doesn't play well post-flop without significant improvement. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

K3o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability postflop. On the button, stealing blinds is possible, but opponents have shown loose tendencies in previous hands (frequent calling and limping). Minni is shorter at 36BB and may defend wide, while Flash is aggressive. Equity is poor if called, often dominated by better kings. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long 150-hand game.

I am in the Small Blind with 9d 3h, which is a weak hand. The Button (Elon) has folded, leaving only me and Minni in the Big Blind. While I could attempt to steal the blinds, the hand is very poor for post-flop play, and Minni has shown some willingness to defend and play aggressively (as seen in Hand #34). Given my stack size and the poor quality of my cards, folding is the most disciplined play to avoid unnecessary losses in a blind-vs-blind situation.

I am on the button with Ace-Jack offsuit (Ac Jd), a strong starting hand in a three-handed game. My stack is quite healthy at 18,400 chips. With the blinds at 50/100, raising is the standard play here to seize the initiative and put pressure on the blinds. Since Minni has a relatively short stack (3,700), a standard 3x raise to 300 should suffice to either take down the blinds or set up a post-flop situation where I have position and the betting lead.

Facing a standard 3bb button open to 300 with Jc6c from the small blind and 37bb effective, this hand is marginal but playable because it’s suited and has decent connectivity. A tight fold would be too nitty 3-handed; a small reraise (to 500) or a larger 3-bet would commit too much with very little equity and will get us OOP postflop against two big stacks. Calling 250 more (300 total commitment including my 50 SB) keeps the pot size controlled, gives me a cheap chance to hit a flop where J6s can realize equity (flush/backdoor straight and top-pair possibilities), and preserves stack depth for postflop maneuvering. Plan: call preflop, play cautiously postflop — fold to heavy aggression if I miss, and extract value/raise only when I connect well.

K4o is a marginal hand at best, offsuit with a weak kicker. Facing a button raise from the aggressive Flash and a call from Minni, who has a shorter stack but still adds multiway risk. I'm out of position postflop against two opponents. Pot odds are ~3.5:1 (200 into 700), but equity and playability are insufficient given domination risks (vs better Kx) and poor implied odds. Folding preserves my deep stack (17.75BB effective vs Minni but deep overall) for the 150-hand game, avoiding tough spots OOP with trash.

I have middle pair (6s) with a Jack kicker and only a backdoor club draw (need both turn and river clubs). Flash opened from the button preflop, so his range contains a lot of Qx, broadways and bluffs but also many hands that already beat me (Qx, bigger pairs, sets). With ~34.5bb effective and OOP, I want to keep the pot under control and let him take the initiative. Checking preserves showdown equity, allows me to call small-to-medium continuation bets (catch bluffs and worse pairs) and fold to heavy aggression or raise only if I improve. If he checks back I’ll re-evaluate the turn and only build the pot with a real improvement or a safe runout.

On a very dry board of Qc 6h 2d, my Ace-high has some showdown value but is better used to take the initiative with a continuation bet. Minni checked, which is expected after calling from the small blind. I will bet 350, which is half the pot, to represent a strong hand and fold out her air while maintaining a healthy stack-to-pot ratio against her shorter stack.

Call. I have middle pair (6) with a Jack kicker and a tiny backdoor club draw (Jc6c). Flash’s flop stab is small (350 into 1050 ≈ 1/3 pot) and, as the preflop raiser on the button, his range is wide — lots of Qx and broadways plus some bluffs and small pairs. Raising OOP with second pair is thin and will bloat the pot against hands that already beat me. Folding is too nitty because this bet is cheap and I have showdown value against bluffs/worse pairs. Calling 350 (total committed after this action = 650) keeps the pot controlled, lets me realize equity, and allows me to fold to significant turn aggression if I miss. Plan: call the turn if the action is modest, fold to heavy raises unless I improve to two pair/trips or pick up a strong draw; extract value/raise only when I have a clear improvement.

I have middle pair (6) with a decent Jack kicker (Jc6c), but the backdoor club flush is dead after the 5d on the turn. Flash was the preflop raiser and c-bet the flop small, so his range is wide (Qx, broadways, some small pairs and bluffs). With no improvement and being out of position, betting here is thin — it bloats the pot and can get me raised by hands that already beat me (Qx, 2x, sets). Pot is 1,400 and my stack is ~3,100; I don’t want to commit a large portion of my stack with a vulnerable second pair. Checking preserves showdown equity, keeps weaker hands and bluffs in, and lets me fold to clear strength (big turn/river aggression) while calling reasonable river bets for value. Plan: check and re-evaluate on the river — call small bets I beat, fold to large pressure unless I improve.

On this dry board, my Ace-high still has decent showdown value. Minni called the flop continuation bet, which suggests she has a piece of the board (like a pair of sixes or a queen) or a pocket pair. The 5d on the turn is a blank that doesn't significantly change the board texture. By checking back, I control the pot size and avoid a potential check-raise from her relatively short stack, while giving myself a chance to improve on the river or reach showdown for free. If she was floating with a worse hand, I remain ahead.

I have middle pair (6) with a Jack kicker — a marginal showdown hand. Villain’s line (button open, small flop c‑bet, check turn) is wide and contains a lot of Qx, broadways and missed hands; the river 3c doesn’t meaningfully improve me (it completes some rare 4–card straights for a held 4, but no flush). Betting here would bloat the pot with a vulnerable hand and either get called by better (Qx, sets, 2x) or fold out only pure air; a check preserves showdown equity and avoids committing a large portion of my ~3100 stack to a marginal hand. I’ll check to see a showdown — plan to call a small river bet but fold to heavy pressure or a pot-sized raise.

On the river, the board is Qc 6h 2d 5d 3c and Minni has checked for the third time. My Ace-high (Ac Jd) has significant showdown value in this three-handed game, especially after a dry run of cards. While Minni called my flop continuation bet, her checks on the turn and river suggest she may be holding a marginal hand like a small pair or a missed draw. Betting now is unlikely to fold out a pair of Sixes or a Queen, and I am unlikely to get called by anything worse than an Ace. Therefore, checking back is the most prudent play to realize my showdown value and take down the pot if my Ace-high is the best hand.

I'm on the button with Q8♦ — a playable, suited Q that has decent blockers and positional advantage against only the SB and BB. With a 45bb effective stack, I want to take the initiative, pick up the blinds, and avoid a multiway limp pot, so I open-raise to a standard 3xBB (300). That size gives fold equity while keeping the pot manageable post-flop; if I face a meaningful 3-bet I will treat my hand as marginal and likely fold, otherwise I'll use position to c-bet and pressure lighter ranges.

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand Qc2d is offsuit, unconnected, low kicker, and one of the worst possible starting hands. With 3 players left, button ranges are wide, but Q2o has very poor playability out of position postflop, minimal equity against calling range, and no fold equity if I raise. Pot odds are about 450:250 (~1.8:1), but implied odds are terrible OOP with this hand. Minni has 4200 stack (42BB), but folding trash preserves stack for better spots in long 150-hand game. Standard fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Jc6s. Minni, on the Button, has raised to 300. Elon has folded. The pot is 450 and it costs me 200 more to call. While J6o is a weak hand, I am getting 2.25:1 odds on a call. However, Minni's stack is quite short (4200 chips), which limits my implied odds if I hit a big hand. Looking at previous hands, Minni is quite active and has shown some aggression. J6o doesn't play well post-flop, especially out of position against an aggressive button. Folding is the most disciplined play here to preserve my stack for better spots, as J6o is significantly below the average defending range.

94o is a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against calling ranges, and little steal value even from the button against blinds. Minni's short stack might shove wide, but folding trash preflop maximizes EV long-term. No position advantage justifies playing this.

I am in the Small Blind with Qc 6c, a suited hand with some playability. Elon has folded from the Button. The Big Blind, Minni, has a relatively short stack of 4,550 compared to the starting 20,000. While Qc 6c isn't a premium hand, in a blind-vs-blind situation, it's strong enough to open for a raise to put pressure on the Big Blind or potentially win the pot immediately. Raising to 300 is a standard open size here to discourage a defense from Minni.

I’m in the big blind with 4♦4♠ facing a 3bb open to 300 from the SB. Pot is 400 and I need to add 200 more (300 total) to call. With ~45bb effective this is ideal set-mining spot: calling preserves strong implied odds and keeps SPR healthy (~7) so a flopped set can earn big value. A 3-bet with a low pocket pair is thin for value and risky as a bluff against a capable opener. My plan is to call, see the flop, shove for value if I hit a set, and otherwise proceed cautiously (fold to heavy aggression on dry overcard boards, or continue vs small C-bets where appropriate).

The flop of Ad Kc 4c is excellent for my perceived range as the pre-flop aggressor. I have a Queen-high hand with a backdoor flush draw. Given that I raised from the Small Blind, I should have many of the strongest Aces and Kings in my range. A continuation bet of 50% of the pot (300) puts pressure on Minni to have a piece of this board to continue. If she folds, I win the pot immediately. If she calls, I still have some equity with the backdoor flush and potential to hit a Queen, though the primary goal here is to take the pot down now.

I flopped a set (4h4s with 4c on A♦4♣K♣). Facing Flash’s 300 into a 900 pot, this is a clear value + protection spot: I want to charge Ax/Kx and deny or charge club flush draws. Calling is too passive (gives draws correct odds and lets hands realize equity cheaply); a tiny raise is too weak. I’ll raise to 1,500 total. That forces Flash to call an extra 1,200; if called the pot will be ~3,300 and the call requires ~36% equity — right around or just above a two-card club-draw’s ~35% by the river, so I’m pricing many pure draws out while keeping A‑ and K‑hands and some club combos in to pay me off. It also leaves ~2.85k behind to extract further on turn/river or stack off if he’s sticky.

On a flop of Ad-Kc-4c, I have Queen-high with a backdoor flush draw. After my continuation bet of 300, Minni raised to 1500. Given that Minni has a relatively short stack (2850 remaining) and this board texture is very heavy with Aces and Kings, her raise indicates significant strength, likely at least a pair of Aces. I am way behind her range, and with only a backdoor flush draw, I do not have the equity to justify calling the additional 1200. My Queen-high has almost no showdown value here, so folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with T4o, which is a very weak starting hand. While it is a three-handed game and I should be looking to play aggressively, opening with such a weak range can be easily exploited by Minni and Elon, both of whom have shown they are willing to defend or raise. Given the current stack sizes and my position, there's no reason to waste chips on a hand with very little post-flop potential. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

K♣Q♥ is a strong heads-up hand and should be opened from the small blind to take initiative. Pot is 150 and I only need to add 50 to call, but the table’s standard open size has been ~300; I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB) to narrow the field, apply pressure to the BB’s wide defending range, and build a pot with a hand that plays well postflop. Plan: c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on ace/king-high textures. If Elon 3-bets a small/standard size (callable, e.g. ≤~700) I’ll consider calling to realize equity; if he 3-bets large or jams I’ll fold to avoid playing dominated, deep-pot situations.

Facing a standard 3bb raise from SB after BTN fold. SB ranges are wide in this spot, often 40-50% of hands. A2s is a profitable defend from BB with excellent pot odds (200 into 400, over 3:1) and strong implied odds due to suited ace potential for nut flush draws, wheel straights, or strong ace pairs. Postflop playability is good despite OOP, and Minni's history shows frequent aggression but also calling stations in spots. Raising has fold equity but risks walking into premium hands or 4bet shoves; calling maximizes equity realization. Folding is too tight for the spot.

I opened preflop with KcQh to take the initiative, but the flop A♥ 9♦ 5♥ is exactly the kind of Ace‑high texture that favors the BB’s calling range (lots of Ax, some 9s/5s, pairs, and floats). I have no pair and only a backdoor heart draw, so my equity vs a continuing range is thin and a c‑bet would often be a bluff that gets called by better hands. With pot control the priority — stacks are deep relative to the pot — I’ll check to avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand, induce bluffs, and see how Elon reacts. Plan: call a small probe bet (~1/3–1/2 pot ≈ 200–300) to realize equity and fold to larger barrels (>~300) unless I pick up a pair or a very favorable read on a later street.

Nut diamond flush draw (Ad blocker) on a board with A♥9♦5♥ (two hearts, one diamond). Excellent equity (~35-40% vs most ranges) with nut FD, backdoor straight potential, and ace high showdown value. Minni checked her c-bet flop after wide SB raise (BTN fold), suggesting capped range: medium Ax, pocket pairs below A, weak draws, or floats rather than strong value like sets or top Ax. OOP, but betting ~2/3 pot denies her equity (heart draws have ~35% vs me but I block nuts), gets thin value from worse Ax/pairs, builds pot for when we hit, and has fold equity against air. Her 4950 stack allows room; if shoved on, profitable call-off with superior equity. Checking risks giving free card to her draws or allowing check-raise, better to take initiative.

I opened preflop with KcQh to take initiative but checked the A♥9♦5♥ flop to control the pot. Elon leads 400 into a 1000 pot (a bit bigger than a small probe). My hand has no pair and only a backdoor heart — it fares poorly vs a continuing range that contains a lot of Ax, some 9s/5s and a few strong pairs; I’m only live against bluffs. I had planned to call small probe bets (~200–300) to realize equity but fold to larger barrels (>~300) unless I pick up a pair or a strong read. Given the bet size, my out-of-position status, and stack preservation, folding is the best play here.

3-handed on the button with 9♣6♦ (unsuited). This is a marginal but playable hand in position — raising gains fold equity against the blinds and lets me play postflop with position if called. Standard sizing here is ~3x the big blind; 300 is big enough to discourage easy callers and small limps but keeps the pot manageable with my ~49.5BB stack. If I face a big 3-bet I’ll fold; vs a single call I’ll proceed with position and pot control. Raise to 300.

6h3d is a complete trash hand offsuit with no connectivity, low cards, and poor playability multiway or heads-up. In small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot), the pot odds are insufficient given the hand's negligible equity against Minni's wide button raising range. Out of position post-flop with no initiative, this is an easy fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Long-term EV positive to muck and play selectively over 150 hands.

I am in the big blind with 8d4d, a weak hand. Minni, who is on the button, has raised to 300. Her stack is relatively short (4650) compared to the blinds and my stack. Given the garbage nature of my hand (offsuit, low cards, no connectivity), there is no reason to defend this blind. I have been playing somewhat aggressively, but this is a clear fold to preserve chips and wait for a better spot, especially against a button open.

Qd3c is a trash hand offsuit with poor playability, dominated potential, and no straight/flush draws likely. On button vs blinds, fold equity exists, but Minni (BB) is aggressive from history and has 50BB stack, likely to defend wide. Pot odds 1.5:1 not enough for call. Raising risks playing OOP postflop with weak hand. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I have been dealt pocket Queens (Qh Qs) in the Small Blind. This is a premium hand. With the Button (Elon) folding, it's just me and Minni in the Big Blind. Minni has a shorter stack of 5,000 chips. I want to raise to build value and potentially isolate her or win the blinds. A standard raise to 300 is consistent with my previous play and provides a strong opening.

Facing a 3x open to 300 from the small blind while I’m in the big blind with T5o. This is a marginal, poorly playing hand out of position — postflop I’ll be IP? (Note: in heads-up I act first postflop) — either way it’s weak vs an opener. Calling 200 more would create a 600 pot, so I need ~33% equity to break even; T5o won’t have that vs a reasonable SB opening range. 3-betting to 500 is too ambitious with this hand and would commit chips with bad postflop playability. Opponent has shown frequent opens, but I need stronger hands to defend OOP profitably. Preserve stack (5000) and fold this spot.

I am on the button with pocket fours. In a three-handed game, a small pocket pair is a standard opening hand from the button to pick up the blinds or play the hand in position. I have been consistently opening for 300 in previous hands, so I will maintain that sizing to remain balanced and keep pressure on the blinds.

Holding Kc Qh in the small blind facing a BTN open to 300. I have ~49.5BB (4950) — KQo is a strong, playable hand that fares well vs a wide button opening range. Calling 250 into a multiway pot leaves me out of position and concedes initiative; a min-raise to 500 is too small to gain meaningful fold equity and invites the BB to join cheaply. I want to either isolate the button or take the pot down preflop, so I make a standard 3-bet to apply pressure while keeping plenty of chips behind. A raise to 900 (~3x the open) maximizes fold equity vs weaker holdings, extracts value from Ax and broadways, and still leaves me room to maneuver postflop (~4050 left). If I face a large 4-bet I can fold without committing a huge portion of my stack.

Facing a button open to 300 from Flash and a squeeze 3-bet to 900 from Minni in the SB. My A♦T♥ offsuit is a marginal hand here OOP in the BB. Minni's 3-bet range is likely strong: QQ+, AKo, AQs+, maybe some broadways or bluffs, but as SB she has position on button and squeeze advantage. ATo blocks AA/AK but is often dominated by AQ+/AJs in her range. Calling 800 into 1300 pot commits ~5.5BB now, with Minni having 41BB effective, leading to awkward SPR postflop OOP where ATo plays poorly (misses or dominated). Button can still call/4-bet. Raising risks isolation vs strong range or multiway. Deep stacks (mine/Flash >160BB) allow folding this spot to preserve stack for better opportunities over 150 hands. History shows aggressive play, but no need to speculate. Standard fold.

Minni has 3-bet me from the small blind again. This is the third time this has happened when I've opened from the button. With pocket fours, I am calling 600 to win a total pot of 1900, giving me roughly 3:1 pot odds. While the effective stack size (around 50bb) is a bit shallow for pure set mining, my positional advantage allows me to see a flop and potentially outplay her if she misses or if the board is favorable. I have folded to her 3-bets in similar spots previously, so calling here helps prevent her from over-exploiting my button opens.

I hold Kc Qh on K♥ T♣ J♣ — top pair with a decent kicker. This is a dynamic flop: two clubs on board give the button plenty of club-draw combos, and the T–J connectivity means A or 9 can make straights. Flash’s calling range to my 3‑bet will include Kx, broadways and pairs/draws; many of those are behind my hand but have outs. I want to both extract value from worse kings/QJ-type hands and deny equity to club and straight draws, so I’ll seize the initiative and bet for protection/value rather than check and allow free cards. I size to ~2/3 pot (1200 into 1900) — big enough to charge draws and get called by worse, but leaving room to fold to a strong check‑raise. If called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (continue for value on blanks, fold to clear strength if he shows heavy aggression).

The flop [Kh, Tc, Jc] is extremely unfavorable for my pocket fours as it connects heavily with Minni's 3-betting range (hands like AK, KQ, KJs, TT, JJ, and AQ). Minni's bet of 1200 into a 1900 pot is a strong lead on such a coordinated board and represents nearly half of her remaining stack, making it likely she is committed to the hand. I have virtually no equity to continue and no profitable bluffing opportunities given the board texture and stack-to-pot ratio. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with A♣5♠ and 60BB effective. This hand is a standard button opener — it has an ace-blocker and decent postflop playability in position. The pot is only the blinds (150) and calling 100 is passive and lets both blinds see a cheap flop; a min-raise to 200 is too small and invites more frequent 3-bets. I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300) to take the initiative, gain fold equity, and play postflop in position. Plan: if I get a single caller proceed with positional C-bets and board-dependent play; fold to a large 3-bet out of line or re-evaluate vs small 3-bet based on opponent tendencies.

Td4d is a weak suited hand, not strong enough to defend out of position from the small blind against a button raise from Minni, who often raises wide but still has position and range advantage post-flop. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (250 to call into 450), but implied odds are poor with this hand due to reverse implied odds and domination risks. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities.

I am in the Big Blind with K5s and facing a 3x open from Minni on the button. K5s is a solid hand to defend in this spot as it has good playability post-flop with flush and high-card potential. Given the pot odds (200 to win a total pot of 650), I have a clear call to see the flop.

On a connected board of 6-4-5, I have middle pair with a King kicker and a backdoor club flush draw. This is a solid hand to check-call with against a button range. I will check to the pre-flop aggressor to see how they value their hand on this texture, as this board often favors the range of the person defending the big blind.

I opened to 300 preflop and the BB just checked the flop. Flop 6♦-4♣-5♥ gives me a pair of fives with the A kicker — a decent, positionally advantaged showdown hand and a natural continuation-bet candidate. By betting I can extract value from worse pairs (4x, small pockets), charge overcards/draws, and fold out pure floats. I also block a number of Ax and 5x combos. I’ll size to about half the pot (~325) to balance fold equity with pot control. Plan: if he folds I take it; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously; if he check-raises big (large enough to commit the stacks) I’ll fold most of the time since middle pair is vulnerable.

I have middle pair (5s) with a King kicker on a 6-4-5 board. This board texture is quite wet and favors the defender's range. Minni's 1/2 pot bet is standard for a continuation bet. While I am vulnerable to overcards and various straight draws, folding middle pair here would be too tight. I will call to see the turn and re-evaluate, as my hand has some showdown value and potential to improve or induce further bluffs.

On the turn 7h, the board is now 4-5-6-7, creating a four-card straight. I hold a pair of fives with a King kicker and an open-ended straight draw (any 8 or 3 gives me a straight). This board is very coordinated and favors the range of the Big Blind defender, but it is also dangerous. Checking is the most prudent play here to see how Minni reacts to the straight completing. If she bets, I have a very easy call given my pair and the eight outs to a straight. If she checks behind, I am happy to take my showdown value or see a free river.

Board is 6♦-4♣-5♥-7♥ and I hold A♣5♠ — I have a vulnerable middle pair with the best kicker.
