Elon checked the turn after the club hit, which is ambiguous: it can be a slowplayed made flush, a medium pair/pocket that wants pot control, or a missed hand that’s giving a free card. Betting here has poor expected value — it will rarely fold out the hands that currently beat me (flushes, sets, two pair) and will bloat the pot with a marginal K‑high. In position I should preserve stack and control the pot size: check the turn to see the river and re-evaluate. Plan: fold to a large river bet if I don’t improve; if I hit a K or J on the river, extract value; if Elon checks the river I can consider a small thin value bet on a safe card. For now: check.

River Tc completes the club flush on a highly draw-heavy board (543cc now 5437cc with Tc, four clubs total). My QcJs gives me the Qc-high flush, which is the nuts unless Minni holds a higher club (Ac/Kc), but her button open + flop c-bet + turn check is consistent with a wide polarized range: medium pairs (66-88), missed draws (A2s/56s now bricked), lower flushes (9c8c/6c2c etc.), or checking strong flushes (but unlikely given turn check). Preflop raise and flop c-bet suggest she doesn't have the nuts often; turn check indicates caution post-flush draw completion on turn (7c added third club). Equity ~70-85% vs her range (blocks J for broadways, Qc strong blocker). OOP first to act, pot 1200, effective stacks deep (her 11.5k, SPR ~9.5). Checking risks giving free showdown to worse (pairs/draws) or allowing her to bluff/value bet thin; induces minimal action from cautious villain per history (frequent turn/river checks). Betting extracts value from her calling station tendencies (history: calls flop/turn/rivers frequently with medium strength, e.g., hands 18,29,44,49,52,54,64,93), worse flushes, and pairs planning to showdown. Sizing: 700 (slightly over half-pot) balances value/protection, invites calls from ~20-30% equity hands (Tx pairs, lower flushes, stubborn draws), denies equity to rivers, while < pot to avoid scaring off floats. Avoids overbet (risks folds from value) or pot (too aggressive OOP). Consistent with patient OOP plan exploiting her aggression while building stack long-term.

Elon bets 700 into a 1,900 pot on the river. Calling 700 to win a 3,300 pot requires ≈21.2% equity (700 / 3,300). I hold K♦J♥ (no club) and have not improved — just K‑high. The river completed four clubs on board, so any single club in Elon’s hand is now a made flush and beats me. On top of that there are all the tens/pairs/sets that also beat a K‑high. Combinatorially a very large portion of a reasonable caller’s range (all hands with a club, any hand pairing the board ranks, tens, sets) now have me crushed; versus a uniform two‑card distribution I’m already around the break‑even mark at best and realistic opponent selection (called flop, checked turn, small river stab) skews his betting range toward the hands that beat me. A raise as a bluff is possible only if I have a strong read that Elon bluffs this line very frequently — otherwise I’ll get called by flushes and tens and lose more. With no club, no pair, and insufficient equity against his riverting range, the +EV play is to fold.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with position advantage post-flop. Qs3h is a marginal hand but playable in this spot for a steal raise due to excellent position and initiative. Pot is 150, min-raise to 200, but standard sizing from SB vs BB is around 3x BB (300 total) to apply pressure. Minni's stack is 11450, she defends frequently from history but folding equity is high with wide button range. If called, Q blocker helps against Qx, and we can play post-flop profitably with position. Folding or limping is suboptimal; raising maximizes EV.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a button open to 300. Pot is 400 and I must call 200 (I’d need ≈33% equity to make a pure call profitable). My hand 9h4c is a weak, offsuit, poorly-connected holding that will be dominated a large portion of the opener’s range and has very poor post‑flop playability out of position. A small reraise to 500 would be a thin bluff with almost no blockers and is unlikely to fold enough of his range to justify it. Rather than investing 200 into a marginal spot and facing difficult OOP decisions (reverse implied odds when I hit a weak pair), I’ll fold and wait for a better, more playable opportunity.

Heads-up on the button with 8♥5♥ (suited). Pot is 150 and I only need to call 50, but in heads-up I want to take the initiative with a wide raising range — 8-5s has decent postflop playability (backdoor flush/straight) and works well in position. The min-raise to 200 is legal but too small; sizing to 300 (≈3x BB) is a standard open that generates meaningful fold equity while keeping the pot manageable given my ~114 BB stack. If Elon folds I scoop the pot; if called I play the flop in position; if he 3-bets big I can fold and preserve chips. Raising to 300 maximizes my EV here.

ATo is a premium hand against a button/SB open-raise range, which is wide given Minni's frequent aggression from late position observed in history (many small raises and calls). 3-betting takes initiative, builds pot with strong equity (dominates Ax worse, broadway, suited hands), denies equity to floats, and applies pressure on her shorter 11k stack. OOP but A-high blocker good for postflop control. Size to 900 (3x her raise) standard for value/mix. Calling risks multi-street OOP passivity; folding too tight EV-. Deep effective stacks favor realizing equity aggressively.

Elon 3‑bet me to 900 after my 300 open. I’m on the button with 8♥5♥ — a suited, connected/gap hand with good postflop playability and backdoor hearts. Pot is 1200 and I only need to call 600 more (900 total), giving me ~33% break‑even equity to continue. Facing a standard-ish heads‑up 3‑bet range (some value hands but also bluffs/suited Ax and broadways), 8♥5♥ has reasonable equity and I have position — so calling to see a flop and use position to realize equity / apply pressure when appropriate is the +EV line. I will avoid 4‑bet bluffs with this hand and will fold to any large 4‑bet or shove; but I’ll call here and evaluate on the flop.

Preflop 3-bet with AdTc as BB vs Button open gives us a strong range advantage. Flop 5d-8s-7h is low, coordinated board with possible 6-9 straights and wheel draws, but rainbow suits limit flush draws. OOP as aggressor, we c-bet for thin value vs her floaty calling range (medium pairs, suited broadways, Ax), fold equity vs pure draws/misses, and to deny free cards to improvers. Ace-high has showdown value and blocks her AA/AK. Her Button call range caps high (no sets likely), includes vulnerable hands. Size 2/3 pot (1200) balances our value (overpairs, sets, two-pair) and bluffs (Ahi, KQ backdoors), charges draws, <1/8 her stack. Checking OOP risks multiway passivity or giving free river cards; betting maintains initiative. History shows Minni calls aggression but folds postflop often when behind (e.g., Hands 55,59,97). High EV spot to bet.

I flopped two pair (8♥5♥) on 5♦‑8♠‑7♥ — a very strong hand heads‑up. Elon was the preflop 3‑bettor so his range contains many strong hands, but on this board very few hands actually beat me (mainly sets: 77, 88, 55, or the rare 9‑6 straight). His bet of 1,200 into a 3,000 pot is modest and can include a lot of single pairs, overcards, and bluffs — hands that I want to extract value from. Calling is cheap, but raising is the +EV line: I charge all straight/heart draw equity, deny free cards, and get more value from worse pairs and overpairs. I’ll make a pot‑sized raise to apply maximum pressure without committing my stack — raise to 4,200 total on the flop. If he folds I scoop; if he calls I’ll keep extracting value on safe turns; if he jams/4‑bets very large I’ll re‑evaluate (he should have a lot of sets or very strong hands).

Facing a massive flop raise from Minni on a draw-heavy 5-7-8 rainbow board with only ace-high (Ad Tc), no pair, and negligible equity/draws (minor backdoor diamond flush draw). Preflop as BB 3-bettor, range includes overpairs/Ax that beat her calling range, but her oversized raise (4200 into ~3150 pot) polarizes to nuts/strong hands (sets 55/77/88, two-pair like 85s/87s/57s, straights 46/69, maybe 99+ overpairs) or rare bluffs. Ace-high blocks AA/AK but folds to turn aggression OOP on completing board; equity ~15-20% vs value-heavy portion of range, below required 25% pot odds for 3000 call into 7200 (post-call pot 10200). Her remaining stack 6350 commits her deeply (~SPR 0.6 post-call), turning into shove/call cooler often. History confirms Minni's aggression includes big value raises (e.g., Hands 18,44,95) but folds to pressure when behind; her raise here screams strength. C-bet was standard thin value/initiative, but fold EV+ to preserve 26k stack for superior spots over 150-hand game.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB with AhKs, a premium hand that dominates most of Minni's calling range. Minni has been calling raises frequently in past hands and defending BB wide, so a standard raise to 3x BB (300 total) builds the pot while maintaining fold equity against trash. My stack is deep (26.4k), opponent's 13.45k, pot 150, to-call 50. Raising extracts value from worse Ax, Kx, broadways. Folding or limping is suboptimal.

I’m OOP (BB) with 3h8c — a marginal, unsuited, disconnected hand that’s often dominated, but calling is cheap here. Pot is 400 and I need to call 200 (total commit 300) — ~33% break-even. Against a button open-to-300 range in heads-up this still has reasonable realization equity, and folding too many hands from the BB in heads-up is exploitable. Plan: call to see the flop; fold to heavy, polarized aggression on dry high-card flops if I haven’t connected, but continue or apply pressure if I pick up a pair, backdoor draw, or good board texture. Avoid bloating the pot out-of-position with a hand that has poor playability.

I completely missed this dry Q-K-6 rainbow with 3h8c: no pair, no straight, and no viable flush draw. I'm out of position vs a BTN open whose range contains a lot of Q/K/A combos that will c-bet these boards. Pot control is priority — avoid bloating the pot OOP with near-zero equity. I’ll check and fold to a standard continuation bet; if he checks back I can reassess on the turn and consider a small stab only if the turn gives a good bluffing story or shows weakness.

Flop is Kd Qh 6c rainbow, giving me top pair top kicker (Ks Ah) on a relatively dry board with no strong draws except possible backdoor flush/straight draws. Minni's check after calling preflop indicates weakness or trapping, but her history shows frequent passive play and wide calling ranges (e.g., Hands 55, 75, 97). Deep stacks (26k effective with her 13k), pot 600. Betting half-pot for value from worse pairs (Qx, Ax, weaker Kx), floats, and draws while protecting against overcards or gutshots. Size 300 maintains pot control and fold equity against bluffs/misses. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs.

I checked the dry Qh-6c-Kd flop OOP with 3h8c and Elon c-bet 300 into the pot. Calling 300 into the ~900 pot requires ~25% equity to break even. My hand has essentially no showdown value or backdoor connectivity (only a small chance to pair by the river ≈24%) and even a pair is usually second-best against a BTN c-betting range heavy in Ks/Qs/As and broadways. Raising as a bluff here is thin — I lack fold equity and would face difficult out-of-position decisions if called. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot: fold.

Heads-up on the button with pocket Aces — the best preflop hand. I want to build a sizable pot and get value from Elon's wide calling range (Kx, Qx, weaker Aces, medium pairs, suited connectors) while still leaving plenty of chips to play postflop. A strong but non-committal open to 900 total accomplishes that: it prices calling hands in, discourages limping, and keeps options if Elon 3-bets. If he calls I'll continue aggressively on most flops; if he 3-bets I can evaluate a 4-bet shove or a call based on sizing. Raise to 900 to maximize value and leverage position.

Preflop in the big blind facing a large 9BB raise from the button (Minni). My hand is 3c5h, a complete trash hand with poor connectivity, low cards, offsuit, and minimal fold equity if I 3-bet. Minni's sizing is strong, suggesting a polarized range of value or a steal, but 35o has near-zero equity against her calling range and is easily dominated. Defending wide here would be spewy given deep stacks (mine 26k, hers 12k). Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. History shows Minni aggressive, but doesn't change fundamentals.

Heads-up pre-flop from the SB/Button position with deep stacks (266BB effective). Qd2c is a marginal offsuit queen-high hand, but in heads-up GTO play, the SB open-raises a very wide range (~45-55% of hands) to apply pressure on the BB, steal blinds, and realize equity OOP when called. Qx offsuit hands like this are typically raises due to the queen blocker and post-flop playability. Minni has shown aggression in previous hands but defends wide from BB. Raising to 3x (300 total) is standard sizing for SB opens. Folding would be too tight, limping OOP is suboptimal. Plan to c-bet flops well-represented in range.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with J♣6♦ — a marginal hand but playable heads‑up. The pot is 400 and I need 200 more (300 total) to see the flop; that gives me ~33% pot odds, and heads‑up I should defend a wide range. Stacks are deep (13,250 effective for me vs 26,350), so I don’t want to 3‑bet bluff and bloat a pot OOP with this raggy hand. I’ll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously: look to continue only if I pair the J or pick up meaningful equity/draws, otherwise control the pot and fold to heavy aggression.

Check. I completely missed this A♣ K♥ 3♣ flop with J♣6♦ — no pair and only a remote runner‑runner club flush (~4% to hit both turn+river). Elon opened wide heads‑up and his range contains many A/K combos that dominate me, so I don’t want to build a pot out of position with air. I’ll check to control the pot and preserve chips; plan is to fold to a substantial continuation bet (anything around >1/3–1/2 pot), but I may call a very small bet to realize equity and see a cheap turn. If Elon checks back I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and consider a delayed small probe on a safe card.

Flop Ac Kh 3c is a coordinated board with A-high and two clubs, hitting both SB raising range (Ax, Kx, suited broadways, connectors like QJs) and BB calling range (Ax, pairs, suited hands). Minni checks, which is standard OOP and invites a c-bet. As preflop raiser, I continue with a polarized c-bet strategy: value betting strong Ax (AK, AQ, AJs, ATs), sets, two-pair; bluffing with flush draws, backdoors, or pure air like this Qd2c. Qd2c has Queen-blocker to strong value hands like AK/AQ/KQ, 2c blocks nut flush draws slightly, decent playability, and low showdown value, making it a good semi-bluff candidate. With deep stacks (130BB effective), a 50% pot bet (300) balances range, extracts value from weaker Ax/pairs/draws, has fold equity against air/medium strength, and pot controls if check-behind needed later. Checking back would be too passive, missing value/bluff opp. Avoid overbetting dry board deep.

The flop A♣ K♥ 3♣ absolutely misses J♣6♦ — I have no pair and only a weak backdoor club draw (runner‑runner). The pot is 900 and a call costs 300, so I’m getting 300/(900+300)=25% pot odds. Versus Elon’s heads‑up raising/continuation range on this A/K‑high board (many A‑ and K‑combos and other hands that currently dominate me), my realistic equity is well under 25% — pairing the J by the river is ~12–13% and the runner‑runner club flush is only ~4%. With deep stacks and out of position, calling a half‑pot c‑bet will put me in many difficult spots on the turn/river and I’ll often be behind. I checked to control the pot and now fold to this sizable continuation bet to preserve my stack for better spots.

I’m on the button (small blind) with 6♦4♥ in a heads‑up spot. Pot is 150 and it’s only 50 to call, but heads‑up I should be very wide and take the initiative with position. Raising to 2x the big blind (200 total) is a standard open size: it maximizes fold equity, keeps the pot manageable with our deep stack (~13k), and lets me play postflop in position. If Elon 3‑bets large I’ll usually fold this hand; versus a small 3‑bet I can sometimes call and leverage position, or defend selectively. Postflop plan is to c‑bet favorable textures I connect with and fold to heavy resistance on dry misses. Action: raise to 200.

7h4c is a complete trash hand, offsuit low cards with poor playability. Facing a button/SB raise to 200 (100 to call into 300 pot), getting 3:1 but position disadvantage postflop. Minni's range is wide but 74o has no showdown value, terrible equity vs her continuing range, and no fold equity if shoving or raising. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Deep stacks but no need to defend this.

Preflop as SB/Button heads-up with 6s3d offsuit, a trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Poor playability postflop, dominated by villain's wide BB defending range. Deep stacks (268bb effective with villain at 130bb) make speculative plays unprofitable. Folding avoids difficult spots and dead money loss is minimal compared to EV loss from playing weakly. History shows aggression, but this spot isn't for bluff-raising trash. Preserve stack for premium opportunities to maximize long-term EV over remaining 38 hands.

Heads-up on the button with 4♦5♦ — a suited connector with good playability and implied odds. With deep stacks and a single opponent I want initiative and to avoid giving a free flop. The table’s common open size is ~3x BB, which gives solid fold equity while keeping the pot controllable and lets me realize equity in position when I hit straights/flushes. Calling 50 is too passive and min-raising to 200 is too small and will invite many cheap calls. I’ll open-raise to 300 (total) to take the lead; I can fold to a very large 3-bet or continue postflop depending on sizing and range.

Heads-up preflop, Button (Minni) raises to 300 (2.5x effective). A6s suited is a strong defending hand from BB: ace-high, suited for flush/straight potential, great playability. Getting 3:1 on a call (pot 400, call 200). Folding is too tight against button steal range; raising OOP with this is unnecessary aggression when call realizes equity well postflop. Standard play: call and see flop OOP.

Preflop call with Ad6d was fine OOP as suited ace with good playability getting good odds. Flop Kc Qs 5c is very uncoordinated for my hand: no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw (diamonds), just ace-high. Board hits villain's wide button range hard (any K, Q, broadway, suited connectors like JTs, suited clubs for flush draw). OOP first to act, no reason to bet into her range with air. Checking to realize fold equity if she checks behind or to induce bluffs, but prepared to fold to bet. Bluff-raising weak, betting out donk too thin deepstacked.

Flop: Kc Qs 5c. I hold 4♦5♦ — bottom pair (pair of 5s) with a weak kicker. Heads-up in position against a caller of my 3x open means Villain has a wide range (many missed broadways, Ax, some clubs, and also Kx/Qx and pocket pairs that currently beat me). I want to keep initiative, charge club draws and get value from worse hands while keeping the pot controllable given my weak kicker. A small c-bet (~1/3 pot) accomplishes that: it extracts from worse, folds pure air, and denies equity to draws without committing a large portion of my stack. Plan: bet 200; if called, re-evaluate the turn and control the pot; if I face a large raise, I’ll fold to preserve chips.

Facing a small 200 bet into 800 pot (1/4 pot) OOP with Ad6d on KcQs5c board. Hand is ace-high with no pair or draws, but board is dryish with club flush draw possible. Preflop called a button raise with suited ace playability; range includes Ax, suited connectors, broadways. Villain's button range wide: bets could be Kx/Qx/value, club flush draws (AcXc, suited clubs), weak broadway, bluffs. Pot odds excellent ~5:1 (20% equity needed), ace-high has ~20-30% vs polarized range (blocks Ad nuts, lives vs bluffs/draws). Folding too tight OOP to minbet; raising air unprofitable without fold equity. Call realizes equity, controls pot, sees turn cheap deepstacked (265bb eff). Plan: check turns weak boards, fold to agg if improves, value bet/call rivers with showdown if checked to.

Turn board pairs the 5h, bricking most club flush draws and adding some straight outs (6-4 but unlikely). Still holding Ad6d ace-high bluff-catcher with Ad blocker to nut flush/ace-high combos. OOP first to act into 1000 pot deepstacked (263bb/127bb eff.). Preflop call and flop call were correct realizing equity at good prices (~5:1 implied on turn). No pair/draw/improvement, board favors villain's button range (KQ, Kx, Qx, sets possible from wide raise/call range). Betting out donk OOP with air is -EV: no fold equity vs capping range, builds pot OOP. Bluff-raising later possible but check preferred to pot control, induce bluffs/checks from medium hands (trips, two-pair that fear KQ). Plan: check-call small bets (keep getting ~4-5:1+ odds), fold to large overbets without reads, showdown rivers if checked to. Maximizes EV long-term in deep cash game.

Turn pairs the board and gives me trips with 5♦ on K♣ Q♠ 5♣ 5♥. Villain checked the turn after calling my small flop c-bet, so their range is wide: Kx/Qx, Ax, some pocket pairs and club draws. With a very strong, but vulnerable, hand I want to extract value and charge club draws while denying a free river that could give pocket pairs a full house. A half-pot value bet (~500 into 1000) is the right balance — big enough to get called by worse (K/Q/A/pairs and many draws) but small enough to keep those hands in. If he check-raises I’ll re-evaluate (but trips call/raise comfortably for value); if he calls I’ll re-assess the river and continue extracting.

Facing a half-pot bet of 500 into 1000 on the turn (paired 5h board: Kc Qs 5c 5h) OOP with Ad6d after check-call flop small bet. Hand remains ace-high bluff-catcher with diamond blocker to potential nut flushes/ace-highs, no improvement but solid showdown value vs bluffs/missed draws (JTs, Axs, weak broadways). Pot odds excellent at 500 to win 2000 (3.4:1 or ~23% equity needed); ace-high realizes 25-35% vs Minni's polarized button range (value: KQ, K5s, Q5s, 55/set; bluffs/thin: missed clubs like AcXc, air). Deep effective stacks (122bb), but check-call strategy controls pot OOP, realizes equity cheaply postflop as planned preflop/flop/turn thoughts. Raising air lacks fold equity OOP vs capping range; folding too tight to repeated small sizing after realizing equity well. Call to showdown potential river, fold to river jam if agg, value bet thin if checked to and improves board.

River board KcQs5c5h3h completes some heart flush draws (but turn was 5h, so missed clubs mostly bricked) and adds gutshots/pairing low cards, but my Ad6d remains pure ace-high bluff-catcher with Ad blocker to strong ace-high combos and diamond flush (unlikely). Deep effective stacks (~122bb), OOP first to act into 2000 pot after check-calling small flop (200 into ~800) and turn (500 into 1000) bets as planned from preflop suited ace defend realizing equity cheaply (~4-5:1 odds each street). Minni's button range polarized after persistent small betting: value (KQ, K5s/55/sets, Q5s two-pair), thin value (Ax/Kx draws), bluffs (missed clubs like AcXc/KcXc, air/high cards). Donk betting OOP thin air/value is -EV (low fold equity vs her range, builds pot OOP); raising later streets unnecessary aggression. CHECK induces bluffs/checks-behind from medium hands (trips like K5/Q5/55 fearing KQ/flush, weak pairs), allows cheap showdown/call vs small value/bluffs (~3:1+ odds typically), controls pot OOP, maximizes EV vs her wide range in deep cash game. Fold to overbet river if agg; call small/medium for showdown value. Consistent with multi-street check-call plan.

I have trips (5♦) on K♣ Q♠ 5♣ 5♥ 3♥. Elon’s line (flat pre, check-call flop, check-call turn, check river) looks like a lot of marginals — Kx/Qx, some medium pocket pairs and missed draws — rather than a heavy full-house range. Holding the 5♦ blocks a number of 5-combos (and therefore some of the full-house/quads combinations), so most of his range is still behind my trips. I want to extract value from those K/Q/medium-pair hands and from stubborn callers without folding them off by betting too large, so I’ll make a value bet roughly half the pot. A ~1k bet into the 2k pot keeps worse hands in and charges any club/river bluffs; if he check-raises I’ll re-evaluate (call smaller, pot-sized/huge jams are much more likely to be monsters). Bet 1000 to maximize value while protecting against only a small universe of full houses/quads.

River 3h completes some weak heart draws but doesn't drastically change board texture (paired 5s already strong for trips/set). Ad6d remains pure ace-high bluff-catcher with Ad blocker to nuts (Ad flush unlikely on clubs/heart board). Check-called small flop (200 into ~800, ~4:1) and turn (500 into 1500, ~4:1) OOP as planned preflop with suited ace realizing equity cheaply deepstacked vs button range (preflop raise wide: broadways, suited connectors, Ax). River bet 1000 into 2000 (~half-pot) consistent with Minni's small sizing pattern; her polarized range: value (KQ, K5s/Q5s trips/two-pair, 55/set), thin (Ax clubs/hearts), bluffs (missed clubs like AcXc/KcXc/JTs broadway draws, air). Pot odds excellent: 1000 to win 3000 (4:1, ~20% equity needed); ace-high crushes bluffs (~30-40% vs polarized range, blocks strong Ax). Deep eff stacks (112bb), but consistent small bet sizing suggests bluff freq high OOP after 3 streets; folding bluff-catcher after cheap realization is -EV (leak: overfolding OOP). Raising lacks fold equity OOP river vs capping range. Call maximizes EV: showdown vs bluffs/value thin, pot control OOP executed perfectly multi-street.

Preflop as SB on the button with AK suited, a premium hand. Deep stacks, single opponent in BB. Standard play is to raise for value and to take initiative. Minni defends wide from BB based on history. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot and narrow range. Folding or limping is suboptimal with this hand in position.

Elon (button/SB) opened to 300. I’m in the BB with Jh7h — suited, decent heads-up hand with backdoor flush/straight potential and a good chance to realize equity versus a wide button opening. Calling the extra 200 (300 total committed) is the correct defensive play: pot odds are roughly 2:1 (need ~33% equity), stacks are deep so implied odds are strong, and I don’t want to 3-bet OOP with a marginal hand. Folding is too nitty; a small 3-bet to 500 is possible but suboptimal out of position. Plan: see the flop and proceed — continue/lead with hearts or pair/strong draws, fold or pot-control on dry, hostile boards against big action.

Check. On 6♦‑3♠‑6♣ with J♥7♥ I have no pair and only a backdoor heart/runner equity. This is a paired board that heavily favors the button’s opening range (lots of trips/pairs), so betting OOP as a bluff is thin and will be called or raised by many better hands. I want to keep the pot small, float against a reasonable c‑bet, and look to realize equity or pick up a pair/heart on the turn before committing more chips. Plan: check; call small-to-medium continuation bets (roughly ≤½ pot) to use position and fold to large pressure, turn into a value bet only if I pair or hit a clear semi-bluff card (heart) or check/defend to see a free river.

Preflop premium AKs raised and got called by Minni in BB, who defends wide based on history. Flop 6d3s6c is a paired, dry board favoring my range heavily. I have AK high with backdoor flush and straight draws, significant showdown value, and block strong hands like AA/KK. Minni's check indicates weakness or trapping, but unlikely trap given board. Standard c-bet for thin value, protection against draws (e.g., 45, 45 suited, flush/straight draws), and to deny equity. Bet 2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value from 77-55/65/Ax/Kx and folds out garbage. Deep stacks allow aggression without overcommitting.

Flop: 6♦‑3♠‑6♣, I have J♥7♥. I checked to control pot with a marginal hand and keep range wide. Elon’s c-bet 400 into 1000 is a small ~40% pot stab. Calling 400 gives me pot odds of ~28.6% (400 to win ~1400). My chance to hit a pair by river is ~24% plus ~3% for completing a backdoor heart flush — total equity is close to the required odds, and Elon's button opening range is wide so I’ll often be ahead or able to realize equity. With deep stacks I also have implied odds and can float to use position on the turn. Plan: call, see the turn, fold to large raise that polarizes to trips/strong hands, and otherwise continue only with a pair, a heart, or a favorable runout.

Board is 6♦‑3♠‑6♣‑6♠ (trip sixes on board). I hold J♥7♥ with no pair and my backdoor hearts are dead on the turn — essentially zero showdown value. Elon was the preflop raiser and c‑bet the flop; after the turn a huge portion of his continuing range (any pocket pair, any 3, or a 6) now has a full house or quads. Bluffing here has very little fold equity and a high chance of running into a better hand or a raise. With pot = 1400 and deep stacks I should control the pot and avoid committing chips with air. Action: check. Plan: fold to any meaningful bet; if he checks back I get to see a river for a cheap card (and can re-evaluate only if I pair or get a heart), but I will not barre l here with nothing.

Board is 6d3s6c6s, an extremely dry board with three sixes, meaning any six in Minni's hand gives quads, and she likely has a range that includes some sixes or overpairs after check-calling flop c-bet on paired board. My AKcc is ace-high with minimal showdown value against her calling range, no pairs, draws, or strong equity. Preflop raiser image allows thin value, but turn massively favors her range. Betting risks getting raised by monsters or called by better; thin value from AK is poor as she beats it with most pairs or better. Checking preserves stack, controls pot, allows free river card, and realizes equity at showdown vs. bluffs/worse like overpairs without six or pure air she check-called with. History shows Minni defends wide but checks here indicate possible weakness, though monsters possible. Optimal to check back given SPR ~10 and hand strength.

Check. The river (6♦‑3♠‑6♣‑6♠‑8♣) leaves me with J♥7♥ and essentially no showdown equity — my backdoor hearts are dead and I never improved. The board trips heavily favor the preflop raiser’s range; many hands that continued will have at least a full house or a pair that will not fold to a bluff. Betting has very little fold equity and risks a call or a raise from better hands, so the prudent line is to keep the pot small: check and fold to any meaningful bet. If he checks back we see a cheap showdown; I will not fire with air.

River board completes with 8c, bringing a possible backdoor club draw but no significant new draws for most hands. My AKcc is pure ace-high with no pair or completed draw, losing to any pair or better. Minni's flop check-call on a paired 6d3s6c board likely includes 6x for trips (now boat or better after turn 6s), overpairs (QQ+), strong Ax, or stubborn lower pairs/draws. Her turn check after bricking most equity and the third 6 suggests possible slowplay of monsters or weak hands that can't bet. We checked turn, pot 1400 SPR ~10 for her. Betting AK has no value (beats only pure bluffs/air, which are rare in her calling range) and poor bluffing EV (her range is strong/capped, unlikely to fold pairs+). Checking realizes equity vs any worse air/draws she defended with, induces her to bluff or showdown worse, and avoids getting called/raised by better. History shows Minni passive checks here; check-back optimal to preserve stack deep into tournament.

Heads-up on the button with A♥3♥ — a suited ace is a very strong hand in this spot. I want to take the initiative, build a pot I can play from position and extract fold equity against the big blind. A limp/call (50) is too passive and gives Elon cheap odds to see the flop; a min-raise to 200 is too small and invites 3-bets or limp–calls. I’ll open-raise to 300 (≈3x the BB) — big enough to apply pressure, leverage my position and the Ace-blocker, while keeping postflop SPR comfortable with our ~14.5k stack. If Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing, but here the standard exploitative open is +EV.

9d4h is a complete trash hand offsuit with poor connectivity and low card strength. Facing a button/SB raise to 300 into a 400 pot, calling 200 OOP would commit us to tough postflop spots with minimal equity against Minni's wide stealing range from late position. History shows Minni raises frequently, but folding weak hands like this preserves stack and avoids variance. Standard fold to maintain discipline and maximize EV over remaining hands.

7h2c is one of the worst possible starting hands: offsuit, unconnected, low cards with no high card value or suited potential. No fold equity or playability justifies entering the pot. Even on the button with position vs BB, standard play is to fold trash like this to preserve stack and wait for playable hands. Pot odds don't matter for such a weak hand; EV is negative long-term.

Heads-up on the button (I’m SB) with T♦7♦ — a suited 10-x has good postflop playability and plenty of equity. With deep stacks and the positional advantage after the flop I should be opening a wide range to steal the BB and build pots I can play aggressively. A standard 2x BB open to 200 gives good fold equity, keeps the pot controllable, and allows me to c-bet favorable flops or leverage position vs a single caller. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll tighten up (likely fold); vs a call I’ll proceed aggressively on favorable boards.

Facing a button raise to 200 in a heads-up pot from the big blind with 63o, a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Pot odds are about 3:1 to call 100 more, but equity against any reasonable button opening range (which is very wide but still crushes 63o) is abysmal (~20-30% at best). No fold equity on a 3-bet, and postflop playability is terrible. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. History shows Minni raises frequently from button, but doesn't change the fundamentals here.

Heads-up on the button/SB vs BB pre-flop. I have position post-flop and posted the 50 SB already. Pot 150, to call 50 more. A3o is a playable hand in this spot: Ace blocker reduces opponent's AA/AK/AQ range, Ace-high has showdown value and straight potential. Standard strategy is to raise wide (40-60% range) from SB/button heads-up to steal blinds or build pot in position. Minni defends wide from BB based on history. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is standard sizing. Limping weak, folding too tight given deep stacks (my 25k vs her 14k). Take initiative.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with A♠3♦. Heads-up this is a defensible hand — an ace with a weak kicker has good equity vs a wide opening range, and I’m out of position postflop so I want to keep the pot under control. I’ll call the extra 200 (300 total) to see a flop, realize equity, and evaluate based on the texture — apply pressure if I hit an ace or a very favorable board, otherwise check‑call and fold to heavy aggression. A light 3‑bet is possible, but OOP with a weak kicker makes calling the +200 the better EV play here.

Flop: A♥ 9♥ T♠. I hold A♠3♦ — top pair but a very weak kicker and I have no hearts. Heads‑up the button’s open is wide, so his range contains lots of non‑A hands I beat, but also many better A‑kickers (AK/AQ/AJ/AT/A9), two‑pairs/sets (TT/99/T9) and heart combos that have a one‑card flush draw. I’m out of position so I want to both get value from worse hands and deny free cards to heart draws. A half‑pot lead (300 into 600) hits those goals: it charges draws, gets called by worse Ax and medium pairs, and keeps the pot from getting bloated when I’m vulnerable. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I’ll check‑control the turn and re‑evaluate depending on the card (fold to large aggression if he raises big, continue for value/control if he just calls), and if he raises large I’ll respect that line and fold my vulnerable kicker.

Top pair top kicker (technically pair of Aces with Ace blocker and weak 3 kicker) on a very wet board: Ah9hTs offers flush draws (hearts), open-enders (JQ, Q8), gutshots. I raised pre-flop from button, giving me a strong range including AA, AK, AQ, AJ, AT, suited connectors. Minni's donk bet of 300 into ~600 pot is small (~50% pot), often representing draws, weak Ax, or thin value like 99/TT/A9/AT. With position and deep stacks (my 25k vs her 14k), raising charges draws maximally and gets value from worse pairs. Folding too weak pre-flop range equity; calling passive, allows draws to realize equity cheaply. Raise to 900 total (3x her bet), building pot while protecting hand. If she jams, can fold to sets/overpairs; history shows Minni aggressive but folds to pressure often (e.g., many fold to 3-bets/raises). EV+ play to maximize stack long-term.

I led the flop with A♠3♦ (top pair, very weak kicker) to charge heart draws and get value. Elon’s reraise to 900 is a big, polarizing sizing — his range is very weighted to hands that beat me (AK/AQ/AJ/AT/A9, T9/TT/99, etc.) with a smaller subset of heart semi‑bluffs. The pot is 1,800; calling 600 requires ~20% equity. Out of position with a vulnerable kicker and facing a sizing that strongly represents value, I don’t expect enough bluff frequency to make calling or 3‑betting +EV. I’ll respect the line, fold the marginal top pair, and preserve my 14k+ stack for better spots.

Heads‑up on the button (I’m the small blind) with pocket sixes — a clear hand to take the initiative. Limping (just completing to 100) is too passive and lets the BB see cheap flops with many overcards; min‑raising to 200 is too small and invites lots of multiway-style defences. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the BB) to build the pot when I’m likely ahead, isolate Elon’s wide BB defending range, and gain fold equity vs weaker hands. Stack depth (14100 effective) gives room to play postflop and to call a reasonable 3‑bet for set‑mining odds; if Elon 3‑bets very large I’ll re‑evaluate (fold to extreme 4‑bet sizing, otherwise proceed cautiously). Plan to c‑bet favorable flops and extract value on runouts where my pair holds up.

Jh4d is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and no high card strength. Facing a button raise from Minni, who opens wide from late position, but I have to play out of position post-flop. Pot odds are 400:200 (2:1), requiring about 33% equity, but J4o has roughly 25-30% vs a wide BTN range and plays poorly OOP with reverse implied odds if I hit. Folding trash preserves stack for better spots late in the tournament with 25k effective.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 72o, the absolute worst starting hand in poker. Despite position postflop and only 50 chips to complete into a 200-chip pot for great implied odds, this hand has terrible playability: low cards, offsuit, no straight/flush potential, dominated by any pair or better. Minni's history shows she defends/raises wide but can fold to aggression; however, 72o folds to any 3bet and struggles postflop OOP for her if she calls. Optimal GTO play in deep-stack heads-up is to fold the bottom ~20-30% of hands from SB, and 72o is firmly in that range. Raising or limping risks getting outplayed or stacking off light. With 30 hands left and larger stack (25.7k vs 14.15k), wait for premium spots to maximize EV and avoid variance with trash. Fold.

Heads-up on the button I should open a wide range. 6s9h is marginal but playable in position — connected with straight potential and good postflop playability. Limping is passive and surrenders initiative; raising gains fold equity and lets me play the flop with position. A standard 3×BB open to 300 is consistent with table sizing and keeps the pot manageable with ~142bb stacks. Plan: if Elon folds I take the blinds; if he calls I play aggressively in position; if he 3-bets big I'll fold this hand. Raise to 300.

Q2 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor high card strength, terrible kicker, and no playability postflop. Facing a standard 3x raise from the button (Minni, who has been aggressive historically), the pot odds to call 200 into 400 are not sufficient given my hand's low equity against her wide raising range. No straight or flush potential worth defending. Folding preserves stack for better spots with 29 BB effective depth.

Aces-Jack suited is a premium hand, especially on the button facing only the big blind in a heads-up situation. With position and deep effective stacks (opponent has 14300), raising preflop is standard for value, initiative, and fold equity. Minni has shown loose tendencies in the history, defending wide and playing many pots, but AJs has excellent playability postflop. Standard raise size from SB heads-up is around 3x the BB to 300. Pot is 150, min raise to 200, but 300 balances range well without overcommitting.

Elon opened to 300 from the button (SB). I’m in the big blind with K♠6♥. Pot is 400 and I need to put in 200 more — calling gives me decent pot odds (~200 to win 600 ≈ 33% equity needed). Heads‑up his opening range is wide, and K6o has enough showdown value and improvement potential to continue. I don’t want to 3‑bet OOP with a marginal, often‑dominated hand; folding would be too tight given the price. Plan: call to 300 total, see the flop, and proceed cautiously — continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy aggression on bad boards or when clearly dominated.

I called preflop with K♠6♥ to see a flop, and on A♣7♥4♦ I have no pair and essentially no realistic draws (one heart in hand + one on board cannot make a flush by river). Button’s opening range contains lots of A‑x and broadways that connect with this board, so I’m usually behind. Out of position there’s no value in bluffing a dry board; I’ll control the pot and check. Plan: check and fold to sizable continuation bets, only consider calling very small probes to realize equity or check‑call turn if the action is weak.

TPTK (Ace-Jack) on a dry rainbow flop Ac-7h-4d. Board has minimal coordination, possible draws are weak (56 straight draw, backdoor flush draws). Minni's BB calling range is wide (Ax, pairs, suited broadways/connectors), and her check indicates weakness or trapping, but value betting is standard with position. Bet 2/3 pot (400) to extract value from worse Ax (KQo/Axo), pairs, and any floats/draws while protecting against turn villains like runner-runner straights or pairing the board. Her history shows she calls stations loosely, so sizing gets thin value. Avoid checking as it gives free cards to draws/improvers and misses value.

Facing a 400 c-bet into a 1000 pot on A♣7♥4♦ with K♠6♥. I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor heart possibility; against a button open/c-bet range I’m almost always behind (lots of A‑x and broadway combos). Calling 400 to win ~1400 needs ~28.6% equity, and my realistic equity vs his c-bet range is well below that. Raising OOP as a bluff is too risky (I don’t block A and can be crushed by one-pair/stronger hands or 3-bet). Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold here.

I’m on the button (small blind) heads-up with pocket fives — a hand that’s strong enough to raise for value and fold equity but not so strong that I want to slow-play. Limping/just calling gives Elon cheap equity and lets him see flops cheaply; raising takes the initiative and is standard heads-up. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) — bigger than the min raise to win the pot outright some of the time, consistent with the table’s sizing, and still small relative to our stacks so we retain postflop maneuverability. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (likely let go vs a large polarized 3-bet), but if he calls we’ll play the flop in position and use c-bets or pot control depending on texture.

4s7d is a trash hand offsuit, low cards, no connectivity or straight potential. Minni's button raise is likely wide given her history of frequent opens and limps from late position. Defending BB with this from OOP is -EV; equity vs her range ~30% at best, poor postflop playability. Deep stacks, fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

Heads-up on the button/SB with K3o. Position is excellent, stacks are deep (25k effective ~14k). In heads-up, button range is extremely wide, nearly any two cards. K3o has a king blocker, live cards, and plays decently post-flop in position. Minni limps/calls frequently based on history, but raising steals blinds often and builds pot with initiative. Standard raise size to 3x BB (300 total). Folding is too tight; limping invites trouble. Raising maximizes EV.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m on the big blind with Tc9c — a suited connector that plays very well heads-up. The pot is 400 and I have 200 to call, so calling costs 200 into a 600 pot (200/(400+200) ≈ 33% break‑even equity). Versus a typical button opening range T9s has roughly that equity and excellent implied odds to hit straights/flushes with deep stacks (my 14,100 vs his 25,500). I’m out of position, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a light 3‑bet; calling keeps weaker holdings in and lets me realize equity postflop. Plan: call to see the flop — if I pair or get a strong draw I’ll apply pressure; on a dry, hostile flop I’ll often check and fold to heavy aggression.

Flop is Kc Ks 5s and I hold Tc9c. I have no pair and only a remote backdoor club flush (need both turn+river clubs, ≈4%) and modest pair-up equity (~24% to hit a T or 9 by the river vs a completely random hand). Elon was the preflop raiser, so his range is heavily weighted toward kings and strong broadways that connect well with this board. Out of position with a marginal hand, I don’t want to bloat the pot or bluff into a range that hits this flop more often than mine. Checking keeps the pot controllable, lets me see how he proceeds, and preserves the option to call small continuation bets to realize equity or fold to large aggression. Plan: check — call a small stab to see the turn, fold to significant raises, and only fire turns if I improve (pair/club) or get a very favorable runout.

Flop is Kc Ks 5s, giving me trip Kings with Kh3d (three Kings total: my Kh plus board Kc/Ks). Nuts or near-nuts; only beaten by Kd for quads or improbable K5/KK. Minni called pre-flop raise from BB (loose-calling history observed), checked flop (likely weak/missed or trapping rarely). Board paired kings + spade flush draw scary, warranting bet for value/protection vs draws/Ax/sets. Position allows control. Half-pot bet (400 into 600) balances value from worse (pairs, draws) and thin value from AK/AQ while protecting. Builds pot with monster; check risks free card/giveaway strength.

I checked the flop as planned. Elon’s bet is 400 into a 1000 pot (~40% pot); calling 400 gives ~28.6% break‑even equity. With Tc9c on Kc Ks 5s I have no pair and only a very remote backdoor club flush (needs two running clubs) and ~24% to hit a T or 9 by the river versus a random hand. I’m out of position and Elon's preflop raising range contains many kings/trips that dominate me, but his small c‑bet also includes a lot of missed hands and bluffs. Raising as a bluff here is too thin (poor fold equity and no strong redraw), so the best play is to call to realize equity, keep weaker hands in, and control the pot size. Plan: call to see the turn; if I pair or pick up a meaningful draw I can apply pressure, but I’ll fold to large turn aggression on a blank.

Turn improvement: my Tc9c paired the Ten on the turn (board Kc Ks 5s Td) — I now have second pair (Tens) with a weak kicker. Elon was the preflop raiser and c‑bet the flop small (400 into ~1000), which is consistent with a wide c‑bet range that contains both Kx/trip combos and a lot of air/weak broadways. Against that range my turned pair is often ahead of his bluffs and many one‑pair/A‑high type hands that c‑bet the flop. I don’t want to check and give him a free river or allow him to take the initiative cheaply; a half‑pot probe will extract value from worse hands, fold out pure air, and keep the pot controllable vs a strong hand. Size: pot is 1400, so a ~50% pot bet (~700) balances extracting value while preserving fold equity and leaves room to fold to a large raise. If Elon calls I can re‑evaluate on the river; if he raises big, I’ll give credit to trips/full houses and fold.

Top set with Kh on a paired Kings board (Kc Ks), very strong, near-nuts. Only beaten by pocket KK (quads, unlikely) or flopped full houses like K5/55 (but she checked flop, improbable for such strength). Flop check-call vs my c-bet suggests Kx (worse kicker), Ax draws, or spades. Turn Td completes some straight draws (e.g., JQ) and adds diamond, but spade flush draw still live. Her donk bet 700 (~1/3 pot) is polarized: value (KT for boat, KQ/KJ) or semi-bluff/draw/value thin. Calling is too passive with monster in position, risks cheap river card or her improving. Raising gets value from weaker Kx, two-pair, and folds pure draws. With her 12.8k stack and pot 2100, 3x raise to 2100 total builds pot without overcommitting, leaves room for river shove if check-called. History shows Minni calls stations with medium strength. Max EV: raise.

Elon 3‑bet my 700 to 2,100 on the turn. The pot is 4,200 and calling 1,400 more gives me ~25% break‑even equity. I currently have two pair (Kings and Tens), which is strong but very vulnerable here: any King in his hand makes trips and pocket 5s (or K5) make sets/full houses that all beat me. Given Elon’s preflop open + flop c‑bet line and the size of this raise, his range is heavily weighted toward Kx/trips/sets and is polarized to value — not a spot where I expect enough bluff frequency to make a call profitable. I also planned earlier to fold to a large re‑raise. Raising or shoving would commit a lot of chips out of position with many dominated/behind cases. Fold and preserve my stack.

Heads-up on the button with A♣4♦ — very playable and a clear open-raise candidate. Limping (calling 50) surrenders initiative and lets the BB see a cheap flop with a wide range; a standard 300 open matches the table’s common sizing and puts pressure on Elon’s defending range. With ~127 BB effective I want to keep the pot constructive: raise to take the lead and allow me to realize equity in position. Response plan: if Elon 3-bets a reasonable size I will often call and play postflop in position; fold to a very large 4-bet. Postflop I’ll continuation-bet dry boards for fold equity and extract value on A-high runouts.

89o is a standard defend in the big blind versus a button raise due to its playability, straight potential, and position. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 more into a 400 pot (2:1 direct, plus implied odds deepstacked). Minni's button raises are frequent based on history, suggesting a wide range we dominate or play well against postflop. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is unnecessary with this hand. Plan to play postflop aggressively if we hit.

Top pair with a decent kicker (9) and a gutshot straight draw to the 7 (for 6-7-8-9). The board is fairly dry: 3-6-8 rainbow, low cards, no obvious flush draws. Minni's button raise range is wide, including many Ax, offsuit broadways, suited connectors that miss or have weak draws. We've defended correctly preflop, and this hits our range well. Betting for value and protection OOP, charging any overcards, gutshots (5-7, 4-5-7, etc.), or weak pairs. Half-pot bet sizes well here to build pot without overexposing to raises. Pot 600, bet 300 keeps fold equity and induces calls from worse.

Flop: 3♣ 6♦ 8♥. I have A♣4♦ — no pair but top Ace and a backdoor club. Elon’s 300 into a 900 pot is a small bet (~1/3 pot) and likely part of a wide heads‑up cbet/float range. Calling is the best line: it keeps the pot controlled, lets me use position to realize equity and extract value if I hit, and avoids bloating the pot with a thin bluff that won’t fold many stronger hands. Plan: call the flop, re-evaluate on the turn — if I pair my Ace I’ll value-bet; if I face heavy turn aggression without improvement I’ll fold.

Turn 5c completes a club flush draw (two clubs on board) and brings gutshot straight draws (e.g., 4-7 for 4-5-6-7-8 or 2-4/7-9 combos), but my top pair with 9 kicker (8c9d) remains strong against Minni's wide button calling range, which includes Ax, weaker pairs (e.g., 66, 33), overcards, and missed draws. I bet flop half-pot and got called, building pot OOP. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs, while betting extracts value from worse hands (77-TT, QJ suited, etc.) that Minni stations with based on history (frequent calls on scary boards). Her stack (12k) supports a bet; mine deep. Half-pot bet (600) balances value/protection/fold equity, denying equity to draws without overbetting.

Board: 3♣ 6♦ 8♥ 5♣. I hold A♣4♦ — I currently have only Ace-high (no pair). Flop I called Elon's small c-bet to keep the pot controlled; on the turn he fires 600. With this hand I have 6 clean outs to make a pair on the river (three Aces + three 4s) ≈24% equity. Pot odds on a 600 call into ~1800 (≈4:1) require ≈20% equity, so a call is +EV by raw odds. Elon’s line (call preflop, small c-bet, turn barrel) is consistent with a wide heads‑up range that includes many missed overcards and thin pairs as well as some value hands — I beat a lot of his bluffs/missed cards but I’m behind his solid pairs. Raising as a bluff is unattractive: I have no realistic draw or enough fold equity to credibly fold out his value hands and I’d bloat a pot out of position. Plan: call the 600, use position on the river to reassess — extract value if I pair an Ace, fold to large river bets when I don’t improve, and call small river bets to see showdown when appropriate.

River pairs the 3, filling some gutshot straight draws (e.g., 4-7 for 4-5-6-7-8) and low sets/two pairs possible, but top pair 9-kicker (8c9d) still strong against Minni's wide button calling range after two streets of calls OOP. Her range: Ax, KQo+, weaker pairs (55-66, 77-TT), busted flush/straight draws (clubs, 47, 24). Paired low card doesn't help many bluffs but caps her value somewhat. History shows Minni calls stations on rivers with medium strength (e.g., hands 13,15,29,34,44,49,52,54,64,75,83,88,93,95,99,102,103,105,113). Pot 2400, bet half-pot 1200 for value/protection, denies equity, builds pot OOP, <50% of her stack so callable. Check risks free card or check-behind; thin value bet maximizes EV deepstacked.

Board is 3♣ 6♦ 8♥ 5♣ 3♠; I hold A♣4♦ — only Ace-high. Elon called pre, barreled a small c‑bet on the flop (300), bet the turn (600) and now bet the river 1,200. Pot is 3,600; calling 1,200 gives ~3:1 pot odds, so I need ~25% equity to make a call +EV. Heads‑up his line includes both value hands (pairs: 6x/8x/5x, trips/sets/quads with a 3, plus occasional full houses) and bluffs/missed floats. I beat a meaningful portion of pure air and missed overcards and my Ace blocks some Ax bluffs, so at this price a call is justified. Raising as a bluff is weak — I have virtually no fold equity and would bloat a pot with no showdown equity; folding concedes good pot odds against a range that will include bluffs. Therefore I call 1,200 to reach showdown and pick off his bluffs while minimizing risk.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 76o. In heads-up play, the BTN/SB opens very wide, around 50-70% of hands, due to position advantage postflop. 76o has straight potential (open-ended straight draws possible), decent playability, and is ahead of many random BB calling hands. Minni has a shorter stack (10.3k vs my 29.5k), but I cover. Pot is 150, dead money from blinds. Limping is suboptimal in SB heads-up; better to raise for initiative and fold equity. Standard open-raise size is 3x BB (300). Minni has been aggressive but also calls light from history. Raising builds pot OOP disadvantage for opponent and realizes equity in position.

Elon opened from the button to 300; I’m in the big blind with 3♣8♠. I’d have to call 200 more into a ~400 pot (roughly 33% pot odds) but I’ll be out of position postflop and this hand is very poorly connected and often dominated by a button opener. A min-reraise to 500 would be a weak bluff with little fold equity and would commit me to playing a big pot OOP with trash. Long-term EV is best by folding marginal, unplayable holdings in spots like this and waiting for a better, more actionable hand or position.

Heads-up on the button with Ks7h — in position and can open very wide. K7o is fine to raise here to steal and to play postflop with positional advantage. The min-raise to 200 (total) is a standard, compact sizing: it applies pressure to the BB, keeps the pot manageable and induces calls from worse hands. If Elon folds I take down the pot; if called I plan to C‑bet favorable boards and play cautiously on heavy resistance. If Elon 3-bets big I’ll usually fold K7o to avoid playing a bloated pot out of position against a strong range.

7c4h is a complete trash hand: low, uncoordinated, offsuit, no straight or flush potential worth playing. Minni's min-raise to 200 from SB/Button indicates a wide range, but my equity is poor against it (~30% or less). Pot odds of calling 100 to win 300 (3:1) aren't sufficient given the hand's playability and my positional disadvantage OOP postflop. Folding preserves my deep stack (29.6k vs her 10.1k) for better spots. Standard fold preflop.

82 suited is a marginal hand at best from the small blind in a heads-up pot. While it has some suited connector potential, the low cards limit its playability and equity against a wide big blind defending range. Pot odds to call the additional 50 into 150 are decent (3:1), but postflop I'll be out of position if it goes multiway or checked through, and Minni shows loose tendencies from history (frequent calls and raises with medium strength). Raising to steal adds risk with such a weak hand that plays poorly postflop. Stacks are deep (mine 29.5k, hers 10k), so folding preserves stack for premium spots in late game stages (hand 128/150). Discipline over speculation maximizes EV long-term.

I have pocket fives on the button (heads-up, SB) with 104bb effective. This is a clear open-raise spot: I want to build the pot with a medium pair, deny the big blind cheap equity with overcards, and keep the initiative and positional advantage postflop. A standard 3xBB open (300 total) matches table sizing, gives good fold equity, and still allows set-mining if Elon 3-bets small. Calling 50 is too passive and folding is too weak. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll strongly consider folding to avoid out-of-position deep-stack battles; if he 3-bets moderately I may call for implied odds to hit a set. Raise to 300.

Facing a standard button raise to 3BB from Minni, who raises frequently in late position based on hand history. TdJh offsuit has solid equity (around 40-45%) against a wide button opening range, including many suited connectors, broadway cards, and Ax hands. It offers straight potential, overcard value, and fold equity postflop. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into a pot of ~450 gives ~2.25:1, requiring only ~30% equity, which we exceed. OOP in BB but heads-up, we can realize equity effectively with deep stacks (effective 10k). Folding is too tight for this spot; 3-betting is unnecessary as we prefer multiway realization. Plan: call, see flop, cbet if advantageous or check-raise draws, exploit Minni's aggression.

Flop of 5h-Qh-6h is extremely coordinated and wet: three to flush, multiple straight draws (34, 47, 78 suited possible), pairs like Qx, 65, 76. My TdJh has J-high (overcard), backdoor flush draw with one heart, but no pair, no strong draw, no straight potential (gutshots unlikely). OOP vs button raiser who connects well with suited broadway, connectors, Ax suited. History shows Minni aggressive postflop, often betting draws/value. No fold equity for a bet/donk; betting would induce raises from better hands/draws. Pot odds poor if bet/call. Best: check-fold to any reasonable bet, preserve stack for better spots. Deep stacks but no equity realization plan. Tight check.

I have flopped a set (5♣5♠ on 5♥ Q♥ 6♥). This is a very strong hand heads-up but the flop is three hearts, so there is some risk Elon already has a made flush (two hearts). Betting for value and to charge heart draws is the right play — I want worse hands (Qx, 6x, smaller pairs, one-heart draws) to call while denying free cards. A 2/3-pot bet (400 into 600) accomplishes that: large enough to charge draws and get value, small enough to keep many weaker hands in. If Elon check-raises big I’ll reassess (range skewed to nut flushes/strong hands); if he calls I’ll continue extracting on later streets.

Facing a 400 bet into 1000 on a highly coordinated flop (5h-Qh-6h) with three to the flush and open-ended straight draws possible, my TdJh holds only jack-high with a backdoor heart draw and no immediate equity like a pair, flush draw, or straight draw. Minni's button opening range hits this board hard: Qx, suited broadways, connectors (78s, 47s), Ah/Kh. Her 40% pot bet represents value or strong semi-bluffs, and history shows her aggression postflop (e.g., Hands 18,29,44 where she bets draws/value). Pot odds require ~22% equity to call, but OOP realization is poor on scary turn/river cards likely to complete draws. No fold equity for raise; check-fold preserves deep stack (29k) for premium spots in final hands of 150-hand session. Folding is GTO-correct vs aggressive button.

Ks8s is a strong suited connector/broadway hand with good playability, position on the BB, and potential for flush/straight draws post-flop. With deep effective stacks (Minni has ~106 BBs), raising from SB vs BB is standard to build the pot, deny equity to worse hands, and take initiative. Historical play shows frequent 300 raises pre-flop. Min raise is 200, but 3x BB (300) is optimal size for value and fold equity against Minni's wide BB defense range.

Elon opened to 300 from the SB. As the BB with 7♥6♠ I'll defend by calling the extra 200 (total 300). In heads-up play the BB should defend wide — 7-6 offsuit has enough connectivity to realize equity and can outplay many SB opens. The call is small versus my 10,650 stack and gives good pot odds (200 into a 400 pot → ~33% break-even). 3-betting to 500 is unnecessary out of position with a marginal hand; folding is too tight. Post-flop plan: play cautiously OOP — check and evaluate texture, continue with strong pairs/straights or good draws, fold to heavy pressure on dry boards where I miss.

Flop: 4c 3h 7s. I hold 7h6s — top pair with a weak kicker but very playable heads‑up. Elon opened wide from the SB preflop, so his range contains many overcards, one‑pair hands (3x/4x), and floats. The board is connected (3–4–7) so there are some straight possibilities (notably a 5 completes a 3‑4‑5‑6‑7), but it’s still relatively dry and my pair is ahead of a lot of his range. Pot is 600 and I’m OOP with no bet to call, so I want to both extract value from worse pairs/overcards and deny cheap equity to straight draws. I’ll make a standard value/protection stab of ~50% pot to accomplish that while keeping the pot manageable. Plan: bet 300; if he calls I’ll continue cautiously on the turn, if he raises I’ll size‑read — call small raises for pot control and fold to or re‑evaluate large, committed raises.

Ks8s has two overcards (K and 8 somewhat live), giving 6 outs (~24% equity by river), plus backdoor spade flush draw (two running spades ~4%) and straight potential. Flop 4c3h7s is rainbow low board, missing my preflop raising range heavily, but as OOP check-call vs BB donk bet (half-pot 300 into 600), pot odds are excellent (300 into 900, ~25% needed). Minni's BB calling range + flop donk includes pairs (22-77 ~12%), Ax/spade draws, gutshots (e.g., 56s OESD, 25/26/58/65), which my hand has good equity against (~35-40% vs polarized range). Deep effective stacks (her 101BB) provide implied odds if hit top pair/top kicker or better. Raising semibluff possible but risks her stack-off with better (sets/pairs/draws), better to pot control OOP, realize equity, and use position on later streets if floats. History shows Minni calls wide but folds to aggression; calling keeps pot multiway potential and extracts from worse. Optimal vs aggro BB donk.

Heads‑up I have top pair (7♥) with a weak kicker on a 4♣‑3♥‑7♠‑T♦ runout. Elon opened wide pre and called my flop bet, then checked the turn — that line looks like a lot of marginal hands/overcards and floats that didn’t improve to a Ten. My hand is still often ahead of his checking range, but the Ten is an overcard so I don’t want to bloat the pot. I’ll extract thin value and deny free cards (notably a 5 that completes the 3‑4‑5‑6‑7 straight for me) with a half‑pot protection/value bet. Size = ~50% pot (≈600). If he raises large I’ll fold or re‑evaluate (small raise I can call for pot control).

On the turn Td, the board is 4c-3h-7s-Td. My Ks8s has King-high with a backdoor spade flush draw (board has one spade) and backdoor straight draw potential, but no made hand or strong draw. Preflop I raised as BTN/SB with a strong suited connector, Minni defended wide from BB. Her flop donk-bet (1/3 pot) into a low rainbow board and my call kept the pot controlled OOP for her. Now turn bet 600 into 1800 (~1/3 pot again) suggests continued aggression with either improved hands (Tx, 7x, slowplayed pairs/sets), draws (spade flush draw, 56 straight, gutshots like 52/65/89/J9), or bluffs/thin value. Pot odds are excellent: 600 to call into 1800 (3:1, ~25% equity needed), and my hand realizes equity well (~30-40% vs her polarized range including bluffs/draws like Axs, suited connectors she defends wide). Effective stacks deep (her ~95BB left), implied odds if I hit K/running spades/backdoors. History shows Minni frequently donks/raises with marginal holdings but folds to aggression later; calling pot-controls, denies her fold equity, and positions me well for river (cheap check or value bet if improves). Raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal equity vs her calling range (Tx+, sets); folding too tight pre-river given live K/8 and playability. Call to realize equity and exploit her aggression.

The river 7c gives me trips (7h + 7s + 7c). I led both the flop and the turn and got called, so Elon’s continuing range contains a lot of single‑pair hands (Tx, some small/medium pairs like 4x/3x, and busted overcards that have been floating). Only a small subset of hands beat me (full houses from pocket 4s/3s/TT or the very rare 7+T combo). This is a clear value spot — I want to charge hands like tens and medium pairs that will call one more river bet. I’ll size for value but not so big that all of those hands fold: 2/3 pot (≈1600 into 2400) balances extraction and call frequency well. If he check‑raises large I’ll put him on a full house and strongly consider folding; if he calls, I win a healthy pot from worse pairs.

River pairs the 7 (board: 4c-3h-7s-Td-7c), missing all my draws (spade FD whiffed, no pair/straight with Ks8s). King-high is live-ish but crushed by Minni's triple-barrel range as OOP aggressor: likely 77 (trips), 7x/A7/T7 (two pair), Tx+ (TP), sets from low board, or polarized bluffs (missed 56 straight, spade draws like Axs/JTs). Pot odds 4000:1600 (~28.6% needed) poor vs her value-heavy calling range (~10-20% equity max). Her sizing ~2/3 pot commits 20% of remaining stack (7950 left), suggests strength over bluffs. History shows Minni bets marginal draws/value but folds to turns/rivers; called flop/turn OOP pot control, but no improvement + paired board worsens equity. Raising bluff (min 3200) risks her snap-call/shove with better (effective ~8k pot post-call/raise huge); folding realizes no equity loss, preserves deep stack (28k/280BB) for better spots in final 20 hands. Standard fold vs triple barrel into paired board.

Heads-up on the button (I posted SB 50) holding pocket deuces (2h2d). Pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to call; effective stacks are deep (~119 BB) so set-mining is valuable but I don’t want to give the BB a free flop. I’ll raise to a standard 3xBB to 300 total (i.e. put 250 more on top of my 50) — this builds the pot when I hit a set, gets value/folds vs very weak hands, and defines the action. If Elon calls I’ll use position to c-bet selectively and play sets/overpairs aggressively; if he 3-bets large (committing significant stack) I’ll fold 22 rather than spew chips, but I’ll consider calling a small 3-bet when the math supports set-mining. Overall this sizing balances fold equity and implied odds for the hand.

Heads-up pre-flop from BB vs SB button raise to 300 (add 200). QhTh is suited connectors with excellent playability, straight/flush potential. Minni raises frequently from button, wide range likely. Deep stacks (28k effective, her 11k), great implied odds. Call gets 3:1 direct pot odds (200 into 600), standard defense vs late position raise. Raising risks too much with speculative hand; folding too tight.

Top pair Queens with Ten kicker on a draw-heavy flop (Ks Qs 2c): strong but vulnerable to Ks, flush draws (spades), straight draws (e.g., JT). OOP heads-up vs button raiser with wide range that includes many weaker Qx, Ax bluffs, and draws. Preflop call was standard for playability; now bet for value from worse (KJ, AT, QJ, draws) and protection. Her range hits top pair less perfectly, blocks QQ unlikely. Pot 600, bet 2/3 pot (400) balances value, denial, and pot control if raised. Deep effective (her 11k), but sizing denies equity cheaply. Check risks giving free cards to draws or check-raise spots.

I’ve flopped a set (2h2d) on Ks Qs 2c. Elon called my preflop raise and now bet 400 into the ~600 pot (pot = 1000). Heads‑up a set is huge — his range includes lots of Kx/Qx, A‑x and spade/float hands, and only rare KK/QQ really dominate me. This is a clear value/protection spot: I want to build the pot, charge pairs and draws, and deny free cards. I’ll raise to extract value and force draws to pay — large enough to get value from K/Q/top pairs but not so large it folds everything. If he jams over I’ll call (my set has very high equity vs his range); if he calls I’ll keep extracting on turn unless a very scary card appears. Raise to 2000 accomplishes these goals.

Facing a massive 5x raise (400 to 2000) OOP on a draw-heavy board (Ks Qs 2c) with top pair weak Ten kicker (QhTh). Minni's button raise preflop was standard wide, but this sizing polarizes her range to strong value (KQ, KK, sets, KQs flush draw) or pure bluffs/draws (spade flush draws like JTs, ATs, or air). My hand has showdown value but poor equity vs her value range (~20-30% crushed by Kx or better) and blocks some of her Qx bluffs. Pot odds 1600 into 3000 (~2:1, need 33% equity), but effective realization low OOP on future streets facing aggression; she'll likely barrel draws or value. Raising commits too much with vulnerable hand; calling invites tough river spots. History shows Minni aggressive but this bet sizing indicates strength. Standard fold preserves deep stack (27k) for 19 hands left, maximizing EV long-term.

Preflop in heads-up on the button with KJo, a strong hand that plays well multiway but especially heads-up. Standard to raise for value and initiative. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a good size, building the pot while maintaining fold equity against Minni's wide calling range. Postflop position is excellent as SB/button acts last. Deep stacks support this play to maximize EV over many hands.

Facing a 300 open from Elon (button/SB) with K♠T♣ in the big blind. Pot is 400 and I need to add 200 (300 total) — the immediate pot odds (~3:1) make a call very cheap. KTo is a strong heads-up defending hand: good top-pair potential and reasonable equity against a wide button opening range. 3-betting is possible, but would bloat the pot with a hand that's not a clear 3-bet value (Elon calls wide and stacks are deep); folding is too tight. I'll call to keep the pot manageable, see a flop, and play postflop with position disadvantage but good hand equity.

I defend preflop with K♠T♣, but on this flop (A♥ 7♠ 6♦) I have no pair and am out of position to the preflop raiser. An Ace on board connects heavily with Elon's opening range, so bluffing OOP is thin — many hands that continue will have an Ace or comfortable equity advantage and will call or raise. With a 600‑chip pot and deep stacks, I prefer pot control and to realize my (small) showdown equity rather than build a bloated pot with essentially K‑high. I’ll check to see a turn and fold to meaningful aggression unless I improve or get favorable action.

Heads-up on the button with KJo after raising preflop and getting called by Minni in BB. Flop Ah7s6d is an ace-high board with some straight draw potential (76), but fairly dry. Minni checks, giving me initiative. My hand has king-high, no pair or strong draw, but good backdoor potential and blocks AK/KQ. As preflop raiser, my range is perceived stronger (AK, AQo+, KQs, etc.), so c-bet for thin value against her weaker ace pairs, floats, and draws, while protecting against her check-raises or turns. Position allows controlling pot. Betting ~1/3 pot (200 into 600) balances fold equity and gets value from worse. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws and loses value from her range.

I checked the flop OOP with K♠T♣ and Elon made a small continuation bet (200 into a 800 pot — only ~25% sizing). I have no pair or real draws, but the price to see a turn is excellent (need ~20% equity to justify a call). Heads‑up the button’s c‑bet range is wide, so calling to realize showdown equity and keep the pot small is the best line. I’ll fold to significant turn aggression if I don’t improve; if I hit a pair (K or T) or get a favorable, low turn that allows a clean bluff/call decision, I’ll proceed. Raising OOP as a bluff with K‑high is unnecessary and too risky here.

I have K♠T♣ on A♥7♠6♦9♣ — no pair and no realistic draws (no straight/flush outs). Elon was the preflop raiser and made a small flop c-bet that I called, so his continuing range contains many A‑x hands and medium pairs; bluffing OOP on the turn with K‑high is thin and will frequently get called or raised. Pot control is priority: the pot is already 1,000 and stacks are deep, so I should not bloat the pot without equity. I’ll check to see the river for free and plan to fold to significant river aggression unless I pair (K or T) or get a very favorable action/price to continue.

Turn board brings the 9c, which completes some straight combinations like 8-10, 5-8, or 10-8, potentially hitting part of villain's calling range from the flop (draws or weak pairs). My KJo remains king-high with no pair, draw, or improvement, but it blocks some of her strong hands (KJ, KQ) and has backdoor equity. As the preflop raiser, my range is ahead of her capped checking range here (weak Ax, pocket pairs below 9s, missed draws). However, she checked flop and called small c-bet, then checked turn again, suggesting weakness or trapping. Pot is 1000, stacks deep (mine 26k+, hers 12k). Betting risks bloating pot OOP for her on river or facing check-raise; thin value unlikely as board worsens for my range. King-high has solid showdown value vs her floats, weak pairs, or busted draws. Checking back takes free river card, realizes equity safely, controls pot, and keeps initiative for river if she bets weak. Consistent with exploitative play vs passive Minni who often check-calls light but folds to aggression when weak. Long-term EV positive by avoiding marginal spots.

The river 8♥ gives me a completed straight (T‑9‑8‑7‑6) with TcKs. Elon opened preflop, c‑bet small on the flop and checked the turn — his range contains a lot of A‑x, single pairs and some medium pocket pairs. Almost all of those hands (Ax, 9x, 7s/6s, pocket pairs, etc.) are now behind my straight and are reasonably likely to call a river value bet. Checking behind surrenders thin value. I want a bet size large enough to extract calls from Ax and medium pairs but not so large that these hands fold — roughly 50–70% pot is appropriate. I’ll bet 600 into the 1000 pot to get value; if Elon raises I’ll re‑evaluate (a raise often represents a Ten or a rare bluff).

Facing a 600 bet into 1600 on the river with KdJc for king-high on Ah7s6d9c8h board. Preflop raiser/c-bet flop, check-check turn. Pot odds are excellent (600 to win 2200, ~27% equity needed). My range is perceived as strong (Ax, sets, two-pair+), and king-high blocks Kx value hands like KQ/KT. Villain's check-call flop/check turn then river bet range: improved hands like A8/A9/A6/A7 (now top pair), 98/87 two-pair, T9/T8 straights, or bluffs from missed spades/diamonds draws (e.g., KQs, 54s, JTs) or gutshots that missed. King-high beats all pure bluffs, worse high cards (QJ, Q10), small pairs (below 9s if betting thin). Against value, ~30-40% equity assuming balanced bluff:val ratio. History shows Minni passive check-calls light but fires rivers occasionally thin (e.g., Hand 49 small bet). Deep stacks, small bet size; calling realizes showdown value, denies her successful bluff equity long-term, and pot control post-call. Folding king-high too tight in position vs wide range; raising no value/protection with air. Call maximizes EV.

Heads-up on the button with pocket 4s (4c4h). This is a hand I want to play aggressively preflop — small pairs do well heads-up because of set-mining implied odds and they still have showdown value. Calling 50 lets Elon see a cheap flop too often; raising defines ranges, builds the pot when I hit a set, and gains fold equity. I’ll make the standard minimum raise to 200 total: keeps the pot reasonable versus a bigger 3-bet, denies free cards, and lets me play postflop positionally. If Elon 3-bets large I can fold or re-evaluate; if he calls I’ll proceed based on the flop texture.

My hand is 4d2s, one of the worst possible hands with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. In the big blind facing a button/SB raise to 200 (needing 100 to call into 300 pot), the pot odds are about 3:1, but post-flop I'll be out of position with minimal equity against Minni's wide heads-up raising range. This hand has no showdown value and terrible playability for bluffs or floats. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid difficult spots.

Heads-up on the button (SB) preflop with Ah2c (A2o). Ace-high hands are strong in heads-up playability due to the ace blocker and potential for top pair/top kicker. Standard strategy is to raise for initiative, thin value, and fold equity against Minni's wide BB range. Limping is too passive; folding too tight late in the game with deep stacks (261 BB effective). Raise to 300 (3x BB standard size) to build the pot when ahead often and apply pressure. History shows frequent 300 raises preflop.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and I’m in the big blind with 7♣7♦. Heads‑up this is a strong hand against a wide opening range. With healthy stacks I want to take the initiative rather than call and play OOP. A standard 3‑bet to 1,000 gives meaningful fold equity, charges broadways and ace‑high hands, and keeps the pot manageable.
